title: Facility Operations Center Engineer
description: **Description** AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. The Data Center Global Services organization is looking for an individual with proven and tested leadership skills to help bring to fruition a newly developed Facilities Operations Center. The Facility Operations Center is responsible for 24X7 monitoring of the data center’s physical infrastructure and will serve as a facilitator for all Large Scale Events. This team will ensure that the customer experience will be optimal and gratifying by way of consistency, reliability and attention to detail. The ideal candidate will need to have an understanding of data center IT Infrastructure and data center Facilities infrastructure and how the two entities co\-exist. The successful candidate will be responsible for providing assistance and support to the onsite team. The position will help ensure overall availability and reliability to meet or exceed defined service levels. Qualified candidates must also have experience functioning in a large\-scale data center and have demonstrated and displayed the ability to think outside of the box. This skill is essential, as the candidate will assist with aligning the Facility Operations Center with the larger objectives of other business and peer organizations. You will need to play a role with growing the team to a standard of operational excellence. If you are passionate about the Customer Experience; you think and act globally; and you want to contribute to the operational excellence of Amazon Data Centers, then this may be the challenge you’ve been looking for! Key job responsibilities Monitors the global data center Amazon facilities infrastructure platform Serves as a Point of Contact for internal and external teams Assists with the development of communication and escalation protocols designed to improve the team’s performance Contributes to outage post mortem analysis Ensures records are updated in a timely manner Assists with coordination and communication during emergency recovery efforts Assists with the development of tools that will enhance the team’s performance Ability to support a 24X7 environment (This is shift work which may require that you work weekends and holidays) **Internal Job Description** About the team Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why flexible work hours and arrangements are part of our culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud. **Basic Qualifications** * Experience in data center engineering or operations * Experience in a data center or other critical environment * Experience in operations and on\-call support for data center facilities, mission critical plants, or production facilities **Preferred Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or a related field * Experience in electrical or mechanical engineering Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner. **Company** \- Amazon Data Services Ireland Limited Job ID: A10418303

title: Fast Food Cook Full Time
description: **We’re Hiring – Full\-Time Chef** Swingyard Driving Range, Elmgreen Golf Centre Join the Team at Swingyard – Part of the Carr Golf Group We’re looking for a skilled, efficient Chef to join our kitchen at Swingyard, Elmgreen Golf Centre, Dublin 15 – a newly renovated, high\-tech golf entertainment venue. This isn’t your typical driving range. With in\-bay dining, a licensed café, outdoor terrace, and cutting\-edge ball\-tracking tech, we offer a fun, interactive experience for all ages. As Chef, you’ll prepare and cook a variety of dishes quickly and to a high standard, ensuring a great food experience for our guests. **Key Responsibilities** * Receive and Prepare Orders: Take customer orders from waitstaff and prepare food according to specific requests. * Cooking: Grill, cook, and fry a variety of foods including hamburgers, sandwiches, pizzas, fries and other quick\-serve items. * Order Management: Plan and manage work orders to ensure all components of an order are ready and served simultaneously. * Food Preparation: Perform basic food preparation tasks, such as slicing meats and vegetables, and garnishing dishes. * Service: Complete orders ensuring it is presented attractively before served to customers and ensure it is presented attractively before serving to customers. * Inventory Management: Order supplies as needed and ensure shelves are well\-stocked. * Cleaning: Maintain cleanliness of food preparation equipment and areas, adhering to health and safety standards. * Wastage: Ensuring that a minimum of food is wasted. **Requirements** * Previous experience as a short\-order cook or in a similar role. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment. * Strong organizational skills and attention to detail. * Excellent communication skills. * Basic knowledge of food safety and hygiene practices * Fully HACCP Trained would be an advantage but if not, full training will be given. * Flexibility to work various shifts, including weekends and holidays **What We Offer!** * 20 Days Annual Leave * 1 Additional Annual Leave Day for your Birthday * Additional Annual Leave with Length of Service * Complimentary rounds of golf * Discounts in Pro Shop * Car Parking On\-Site * Social Club * Annual Salary Review * Cycle to Work Scheme Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Store discount Work Location: In person

title: Field Technician & Operations Associate
description: **Speed Queen Self Service Laundry – Ireland \& UK** **Location:** Dublin (WeWork, 2 Dublin Landings) \+ travel across Ireland **Type:** Full\-time **About Us** Speed Queen Self Service Laundry is part of DNL Collective, a growing group focused on building and scaling operationally strong, multi\-site businesses across Ireland and the UK. Our laundry division is one of the fastest\-growing self\-service operators in Europe, with 40\+ locations and continued expansion underway into the UK. We build and operate high\-quality launderette locations with a focus on reliability, efficiency, and customer experience. As we scale, we’re looking for ambitious individuals who want to grow with the business and take on real responsibility early. **The Role** This is a hands\-on, fast\-paced role combining field\-based technical work with operational and project support. You’ll split your time between: * Working on\-site across our launderette locations (installations, maintenance, troubleshooting) * Supporting the rollout of new stores * Coordinating operational tasks and projects You’ll work closely with the Operations team and Senior Technician, gaining exposure to both technical problem\-solving and how a multi\-site business is run and scaled. repair of laundry equipment across multiple sites * Carry out basic troubleshooting and fault finding (full training provided) * Work alongside the Senior Technician to diagnose and resolve issues * Develop the ability to independently handle technical fixes over time **Operations \& Project Support** * Support new store fit\-outs and openings * Track equipment orders and manage inventory of machines and parts * Help maintain project timelines and track progress * Assist with issue resolution across multiple locations * Maintain accurate records and reporting using internal systems **General** * Travel to sites across Ireland (typically between 2\-4 days per week depending on projects) * Spend 2–3 days per week in the Dublin office (WeWork, 2 Dublin Landings) * Support the wider operations team in a fast\-moving environment **What We’re Looking For** * Practical, hands\-on mindset with an interest in technical work * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail * Problem\-solving attitude and willingness to learn quickly * Comfortable using digital tools and systems, particularly Microsoft Excel for tracking, * reporting, and operational tasks * Self\-starter who can take initiative and work independently * Flexible and adaptable in a fast\-moving environment * Full, clean driving licence (required) * Willingness to travel across Ireland * Experience in a across Ireland and into the UK * A fast\-paced, supportive environment with real responsibility from day one Pay: €33,000\.00\-€40,000\.00 per year Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: FinOps Engineer - 12 Month FTC
description: **Sustainability that means business** **Who We Are** Sustainability software specialist, AMCS, is headquartered in Ireland, with offices in Europe, the USA, and Australasia. With over 1,300 highly\-skilled employees across 22 countries, we specialize in delivering technology solutions to facilitate a carbon neutral future. **What We Do** Our innovative SaaS solutions increase efficiency and boost sustainability in resource\-intensive industries. Over 5,000 customers across 23 countries already benefit from our Performance Sustainability software, ensuring we deliver practical solutions for improved profitability and environmental resilience across the globe. **Our People** AMCS offers team members more than just a job, but an opportunity to map out a career with a company that is growing, evolving and setting out new ways of working that are having a positive impact on the world around us. AMCS was established in Ireland and holds onto those local roots and ‘start\-up’ mentality with a culture of connection. Connection to our work, our customers, our colleagues and our community that creates a working environment that fosters openness, collaboration and creativity. **Role Purpose** The FinOps Engineer will drive financial accountability, cost optimization and operational efficiency across cloud platforms within the Cloud Operations organization. This role ensures visibility into cloud spend, implements cost\-effective provisioning strategies, and balances financial efficiency with engineering agility. **Key Responsibilities** * Develop and execute a comprehensive FinOps roadmap aligned with organizational goals. * Establish governance policies for resource utilization, provisioning, and budget approvals. * Financial Accountability * Collaborate with Finance, Product, and Engineering teams to ensure cost transparency and budgeting discipline. * Manage vendor relationships (cloud providers, software suppliers), raise purchase orders, and ensure timely invoice processing. * Operational Excellence * Design and implement a FinOps observability platform to monitor cloud costs and usage. * Integrate cloud cost management tools into the SDLC. * Define and track KPIs for cloud cost optimization and efficiency. * Implement policies for instance rightsizing, resource provisioning, and decommissioning unused resources. * Drive automation initiatives for cost efficiency across cloud environments. * Education \& Awareness * Promote FinOps best practices across engineering teams. * Conduct training sessions to improve cost awareness and financial literacy within technical teams. **Required Skills \& Experience** * Proven experience in FinOps, Cloud Operations, or Cloud Financial Management. * Strong understanding of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and associated cost models. * Experience with cost management tools. * Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills. * Ability to design governance frameworks and implement automation for cost optimization. **Preferred Qualifications** * FinOps Certified Practitioner or similar certification. * Background in DevOps, SRE, or Cloud Architecture. * Familiarity with budgeting and financial processes in large organizations

title: Financial Accountant
description: Elgin Values **Core Behavioural Responsibilities For All Employees** **Agility** We Lead with Agility **Integrity** We Act with Integrity **Excellence** We Empower Excellence **Care** We Value Care **Job Description** **Location: Dublin Contract Type: 12\-Month Fixed\-Term Contract** **Ready to bring your financial accounting expertise into a purpose\-driven environment?** Elgin Energy is looking for a proactive Financial Accountant to support core finance operations and help drive accurate, insightful financial performance across our renewable energy business. This is a 12\-month fixed\-term opportunity to join a collaborative finance team, where you’ll play a key role in financial reporting, analysis, and compliance. You’ll gain exposure to a dynamic environment while contributing to the delivery of impactful solar and storage projects. **What You Will Do:** * Manage the day\-to\-day finance functions, including reviewing bank reconciliations and overseeing intercompany transactions. * Prepare and review month\-end accounts, forecasts, and budgets. * Produce consolidated quarterly management accounts with supporting financial analysis and commentary. * Contribute to internal and external reporting requirements, ensuring timely and accurate delivery. * Prepare and review VAT returns for Ireland and the UK. * Coordinate and prepare annual audit packs for external auditors. * Respond to ad hoc queries from both internal and external stakeholders. * Collaborate closely with various business units across the Elgin Group to support financial operations and process improvements. Your profile * ACA/ACCA qualification with 2\+ years post\-qualified experience (PQE). * Proven experience in management accounts preparation and consolidation across multiple jurisdictions (highly desirable). * Strong foundation in financial accounting and reporting. * Experience in tax compliance (Ireland and/or UK). * Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and strong general IT literacy. * Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate across teams. * Self\-motivated, proactive, and capable of working independently as part of a growing finance team. **About Us** *Elgin is a fully integrated, utility\-scale solar and storage Independent Power Producer, developing, building, owning and operating renewable energy assets across the UK, Ireland, Germany and Italy. With a 10GW\+ pipeline spanning solar, co\-located and standalone storage projects, Elgin brings projects from origination through development to energisation and long\-term operation.* *The company has delivered over 1GW of ready\-to\-build projects, with more than 200MW currently under construction across the UK and Ireland. Elgin is backed by Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners (CIP), which acquired a majority stake in 2024, supporting the company’s long\-term growth and ambition to scale.* *Founded in 2009, Elgin operates from offices in Dublin, London, Ulm and Rome, with a team of over 100 professionals driving the delivery of large\-scale renewable energy projects. For more information, visit www.elgin.com.*

title: Financial Accountant (Newly Qualified)
description: **About The Role** **Who Are We?** At Panda, Ireland's largest Waste Management business, we invite you to join our Team and experience a career that champions environmental excellence while harmonising tradition and innovation. Be part of a team that values strong connections and supports one another. At Panda, we are committed to leading the way in waste management and recycling solutions. Embrace our innovative spirit as we work together to preserve our environment and create a greener future for generations to come. **Now Let Us Tell You About The Role** * Reporting of divisional Profit \& Loss Accounts within month end deadlines * Responsible for the production and reconciliation of all relevant Balance Sheet accounts. * Preparation of month end journals re accruals and prepayments * Reporting explanation of P\&L variances Vs budget * Assisting P\&L owners with understanding of results and variances Vs budget * Ensure all assigned g/l reconciliations are prepared on a timely basis within set deadlines. * Identify and escalate any problems/issues with reconciliations as they occur. * Recommend any improvements in procedures etc to improve efficiency of completing tasks. * Year End Statutory Reporting and Bank obligation reporting * ACA / ACCA Qualification **What We Are Looking For** * ACA / ACCA Qualified * Previous experience in financial accounting or audit environment * Good understanding of financial reporting and month end processes * Experience preparing balance sheet reconciliations and journals * Ability to analyse financial information and explain variances * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Ability to work to deadlines in a fast\-paced environment * Good communication and stakeholder management skills * Experience with ERP/accounting systems would be an advantage * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Positive and proactive approach to work **About You** About Us **We are Panda. We value waste.** We’re in the business of waste management but that doesn’t just mean collecting bins. Behind every collection is a passionate team of people finding smarter, safer, and more sustainable ways to recycle and repurpose materials that would otherwise go to waste. Over the past 30 years, Panda has grown into a leading resource recovery business, bringing together almost 3,000 people across multiple brands, united by a shared vision and strong values. While our operations are diverse, our purpose is clear: to protect our people, support our customers, and create positive impact for the communities and environments we serve. At Panda, **people are at the heart of everything we do** . We work as one team, combining different skills, experiences, and perspectives to achieve shared goals. We care for each other’s wellbeing, encourage development, and support growth because we know we’re stronger together. We expect **excellence** in how we operate. High standards, bold thinking, and continuous improvement drive our passion for innovation. We challenge the ordinary, put safety first, and embrace new ideas while delivering practical, cost\-effective solutions that move our business forward. We **own what we do** . From day\-to\-day tasks to major projects, we take pride in our work and responsibility for the outcome. We don’t walk past problems we address them together. Our expertise, reliability, and care reflect who we are and the difference we make. And above all, we act with **trust and integrity** . Trust is built through open communication, honesty, and accountability. It allows us to move quickly, make confident decisions, and deliver results — knowing our people are empowered to do the right thing. Our journey depends on talented, committed people who want to make an impact. **Take the first step and join us on the journey.** *Panda is committed to attracting and retaining a skilled and diverse workforce that reflects the communities in which we operate and the people we serve. Embrace our innovative spirit as we work together to preserve our environment and create a greener future for generations to come. **Now Let Us Tell You About The Role** * Reporting of divisional Profit \& Loss Accounts within month end deadlines * Responsible for the production and reconciliation of all relevant Balance Sheet accounts. * Preparation of month end journals re accruals and prepayments * Reporting explanation of P\&L variances Vs budget * Assisting P\&L owners with understanding of results and variances Vs budget * Ensure all assigned g/l reconciliations are prepared on a timely basis within set deadlines. * Identify and escalate any problems/issues with reconciliations as they occur. * Recommend any improvements in procedures etc to improve efficiency of completing tasks. * Year End Statutory Reporting and Bank obligation reporting **What We Are Looking For** * Part\-qualified ACA / ACCA accountant * Previous experience in a finance or accounting role * Good understanding of month end processes and reconciliations * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Good communication and interpersonal skills * Ability to work to deadlines in a fast\-paced environment * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team * Experience with accounting systems would be an advantage * Positive and proactive approach to work **About You** About Us **We are Panda. We value waste.** We’re in the business of waste management but that doesn’t just mean collecting bins. Behind every collection is a passionate team of people finding smarter, safer, and more sustainable ways to recycle and repurpose materials that would otherwise go to waste. Over the past 30 years, Panda has grown into a leading resource recovery business, bringing together almost 3,000 people across multiple brands, united by a shared vision and strong values. While our operations are diverse, our purpose is clear: to protect our people, support our customers, and create positive impact for the communities and environments we serve. At Panda, **people are at the heart of everything we do** . We work as one team, combining different skills, experiences, and perspectives to achieve shared goals. We care for each other’s wellbeing, encourage development, and support growth because we know we’re stronger together. We expect **excellence** in how we operate. High standards, bold thinking, and continuous improvement drive our passion for innovation. We challenge the ordinary, put safety first, and embrace new ideas while delivering practical, cost\-effective solutions that move our business forward. We **own what we do** . From day\-to\-day tasks to major projects, we take pride in our work and responsibility for the outcome. We don’t walk past problems we address them together. Our expertise, reliability, and care reflect who we are and the difference we make. And above all, we act with **trust and integrity** . Trust is built through open communication, honesty, and accountability. It allows us to move quickly, make confident decisions, and deliver results — knowing our people are empowered to do the right thing. Our journey depends on talented, committed people who want to make an impact. **Take the first step and join us on the journey.** *Panda is committed to attracting and retaining a skilled and diverse workforce that reflects the communities in which we operate and the people we serve. (DE\&I Policy Statement)* Apply Share * Share Vacancy on Facebook * Share Vacancy on X * Share Vacancy on LinkedIn * Share Vacancy on WhatsApp * Send Vacancy to a friend

title: Financial Accountant - Start up
description: A rapidly growing healthcare group is seeking to appoint a hands\-on Financial Accountant to join its finance team ahead of the launch of a new specialist centre in Dublin City. This is a rare opportunity to join a highly ambitious healthcare venture at an early stage and play a key role in helping build and shape the finance function from the ground up. Working closely with an experienced Finance Director, this role will suit someone who enjoys variety, thrives in a fast\-paced environment, and wants strong exposure across bookkeeping, management accounts, process improvement, and operational finance. **Key Responsibilities:** * Assist with the preparation of monthly management accounts across multiple entities * Post month\-end journals including accruals, prepayments, payroll and intercompany entries * Prepare balance sheet reconciliations and control account reviews * Assist with financial analysis and ongoing process improvements * Work closely with senior leadership on finance projects and operational improvements * Support and mentor junior finance team members as the function grows * Build strong relationships across the wider business **Experience:** * Qualified Accountant ACA, ACCA, CIMA or CPA * Excellent attention to detail and organisational skills * Strong communicator with the ability to work closely with non\-finance stakeholders **Strong base salary and package attached.** **How to apply:** Contact Sinéad Canning on 086 800 6785 for more information about this position. title: Financial Controller

title: Finishing Foreperson

We are currently recruiting a Finishing Foreperson for our residential project in Mooretown, North County Dublin. **Responsibilities:** * Liaise and communicate closely with the design team, Site Manager and other stakeholders * Coordinate the daily resourcing and control of site labour and ordering of materials, completing the online daily site diary and relevant weekly documentation in accordance with company policy * Provide key point of contact for purchasers * Liaise closely with Sales * Coordinate snagging crews, overseeing effective completion of snag lists * Carry out company title: Full Stack Engineer
description: **EXL (NASDAQ: EXLS)** is a global data and artificial intelligence ("AI") company that offers services and solutions to reinvent client business models, drive better outcomes and unlock growth with speed. EXL harnesses the power of data, AI, and deep industry knowledge to transform businesses, including the world’s leading corporations in industries including insurance, healthcare, banking and financial services, media and retail, among others. EXL was founded in 1999 with the core values of innovation, collaboration, excellence, integrity and respect. We are headquartered in New York and have more than 60,000 employees spanning six continents. For more information, visit www.exlservice.com. **Role Title:** Full Stack Engineer **BU/Segment:** Digital **Location:** Dublin, Republic of Ireland (Flexible hybrid working) **Employment Type:** Permanent **As part of your duties, you will be responsible for:** * Design, develop, and maintain full\-stack applications using Python (backend) and React.js (frontend). * Build and deploy Agentic AI solutions, including multi\-agent orchestrations and decision\-making systems. * Evaluate the latest Agentic AI design patterns and make recommendations on their adoption. * Implement features for Agentic AI evaluation, observability and performance tracking. * Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues across the full stack. * Work closely with product managers, designers, and other engineers to deliver high\-quality solutions. * Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code that prioritizes security and data protection. * Maintain high software quality standards within the team by adhering to best practices. Identify and encourage areas for growth and improvement within the group. * Work in an agile delivery/ DevOps methodology to deliver enterprise\-grade production implementation in iterative sprints. * Explore and leverage AI tools to help improve innovation, efficiency and quality across the SDLC. **Qualifications and experience we consider to be essential for the role:** * Minimum 3\-5 Years of experience in application development \& support. * Strong proficiency in Python with experience in frameworks such as FastAPI, Django, or Flask * Advanced React.js skills including hooks, state management (Redux/Context API), and modern JavaScript/TypeScript * Experience with RESTful API design and development * Experience with AI/ML libraries such as LangChain, OpenAI Agents, Strands Agents, or similar frameworks * Experience with containerization techniques (Kubernetes, Docker) * Minimum 2 Year of experience in developing applications running on AWS or Azure or GCP * Strong knowledge of algorithms, design patterns, and fundamental computer science concepts \& data structures **Skills and Personal attributes we would like to have:** * Strong communication skills and ability to work with various stakeholders across various geographies * Master's or bachelor's degree, preferably from an Engineering Background * Be a self\-motivated, curious, and productive under high pressure **As part of a leading global Data and AI company, you can look forward to:** * A competitive salary with a generous bonus, private healthcare, life assurance at 4 x your annual salary, income protection insurance, and a rewarding pension. * At **EXL** , we are committed to providing our employees with the tools and resources they need to succeed and excel in their careers. We offer a wide range of professional and personal development opportunities. We also support a range of learning initiatives that allow our employees to build on their existing skills and knowledge. From online courses to seminars and workshops, our employees have the opportunity to enhance their skills and stay up to date with the latest trends and technologies. * As an Equal Opportunity Employer, **EXL** is committed to diversity. Our company does not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, age, or disability status. * At **EXL** , we offer a flexible hybrid working model that allows employees to live a balanced, healthy lifestyle while strengthening our culture of collaboration. **To be considered for this role, you must already be eligible to work in the Republic of Ireland.**

title: Full-Stack Developer
description: Software Development Europe Region DUB0000UL **Full\-Stack Developer** **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin or Galway.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. We're looking for a permanent, full\-time Full\-Stack Developer to join our Software team to work on real\-world, high\-impact engineering and sustainability problems. **The Opportunity** We are seeking an experienced Full\-Stack Developer to work on multiple active projects in a client\-facing engineering team. **As a Full\-Stack Developer at Arup, you will:** * Design, build, and maintain web applications (both server\-side\-rendering and SPA) * Collaborate with team members and stakeholders to deliver project requirements * Implement automation scripts and integrations where needed * Contribute to architecture and technical decisions * Work across multiple projects and adapt to new technologies **Does this role inspire you?** These are essential skills and qualifications for the position: * Production experience with React.JS or Angular * Strong Node.js backend experience * Working knowledge of SQL and relational databases * Experience with serverless (AWS Lambda or Azure Functions) * Strong communication and collaboration skills for direct interaction with business stakeholders * Comfortable switching between multiple projects and learning new tools/technologies **We also value:** * Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS/Azure), CI/CD, devOps tooling * Experience writing automation tools/scripts in Python * Exposure to microservices or distributed systems * Experience with express\+ejs **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust\-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Benefits that work for you:** * **Professional Growth \& Development**: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and mentorship to enhance your skills and advance your career at Arup. * **Global Opportunities**: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * **Financial Well\-being \& Ownership**: Receive a competitive salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing programme. * **Wellbeing and Culture:** At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * **Equality, Diversity and Inclusion:** We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to building a workplace that reflects the rich diversity of the world around us. We actively promote a culture of inclusion where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Full-Time Experienced Baker / Pastry Chef

title: Fund Accounting & Administration, Officer
description: The **Officer** position is within the *Private Equity \& Real Estate Fund Services* (PERA) Fund Accounting Business Unit of the State Street Alternative Investment Services Group. The **Officer** will interact with all levels of professionals, internally and externally. Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills are required. Candidates must demonstrate initiative, be able to perform well under pressure and simultaneously handle multiple assignments The **Officer** will be responsible for ensuring team compliance with operating policies, procedures and guidelines in the completion of Net Asset Valuations (NAV) across our Private Equity/Debt Fund Structures. The **Officer** will be responsible for team development, staff management and enhancing client satisfaction. This position offers the successful candidate the opportunity to demonstrate leadership, communication and technical expertise. The successful candidate will be responsible for implementing company policy, overseeing client deliverables and contributing to the development of the team and the organization. **Primary Job Duties And Responsibilities** * Direct and indirect management/supervision of PERA Fund Accounting staff * Supervise and review the work performed by PERA Fund Accounting staff, in Ireland and other locations as applicable * Manage internal workflows to ensure internal and client deadlines are met * Manage expectations with client contacts * Prepare and/or review monthly, quarterly and annual financial work papers; including portfolio schedules, accrual schedules, profit \& loss schedules, and partner capital allocations * Prepare and/or review investor capital call and distribution calculations; including the review of investor notices * Process Fund Sub\-Closes on accounting systems or accounting workpapers * Prepare and/or review periodic bank and cash reconciliations and post journal entries * Prepare and/or review quarterly management fee calculations, and other Fund calculations as applicable * Prepare and/or review client related correspondence * Coordinate annual audit with Fund Auditors * Prepare and/or review Fund Waterfall calculations and Partner Capital Account allocations * Review Fund governing documents, including Limited Partnership Agreements and Offering Memoranda, to ensure consistency with accounting procedures and calculations in use * Establish and maintain working relationships with key client contacts, including investment professionals, investors, auditors, lawyers and banking personnel * Participate in ad\-hoc client/internal projects as required * Participate in the development, testing and implementation of new systems and products * Coordinate client deliverables, both with clients and internally, such as financial statements, capital calls and distribution notices * Review and approve all transactions (money movement, trades, etc.) in accordance with established guidelines * Assist team in ensuring all accounting procedures and calculations are consistent with governing documents * Meet all requirements for the performance planning process including goal setting, regular and immediate feedback and submission of formalized performance appraisals. Ensure adherence to internal policies and procedures * Ensure adherence to internal policies and procedures * Assist with planning and management of ah\-hoc client and corporate project work * Co\-ordinate and implement training and development activities across the team * Assist with recruitment of new staff * Manage absences, time keeping and holiday/cover arrangements within the teams * Set and maintain standards of personal and professional performance and behavior; ensuring staff accountability and adherence to company policies and procedures * Schedule and conduct team meetings * Provide coaching, guidance and support to staff on technical, professional and client issues * Manage the administration billing process * Review and delivery of client KPIs **Requirements** * Candidates must have a University degree (an Accounting degree strongly preferred; Business, Mathematics, Finance or Economics degree required) * Prior accounting system experience required. * ACA/ACCA qualification is desirable, but not required. * Private Equity, Private Debt, Hedge Fund or Real Estate experience desirable * Previous Fund Accounting or corporate accounting experience, including supervisory experience * Strong computer skills, especially MS Excel/MS Word/MS PowerPoint * Strong numerical, analytical and problem solving skills * Strong written \& verbal communication skills * Ability to work under pressure and to tight deadlines * Proactive and able to work independently and as part of a team * Demonstrated managerial and leadership skills * Proven ability to build, train and manage effective teams **Why you should join us** * Generous holiday entitlements, including additional Birthday Day Off and Paid Volunteer Days * Supportive and encouraged hybrid work\-life balance * Expansive inclusion, diversity and equity programme * Private medical insurance, health screenings, flu vaccinations and eye testing * Employee wellbeing and mental health support programme * Parental and caretaker support * Attractive pension plan * Life assurance and long\-term disability support * Multiple local community volunteering opportunities * Profit sharing scheme **About State Street** Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future. description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Employee Success Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Futureforce EMEA Programs \& Events Contractor, Job Spec **Role:** Futureforce EMEA Programs \& Events Contractor **Location:** Dublin **Reports to:** Senior Manager, Programs \& Events, Futureforce International **About Salesforce \& Futureforce** ----------------------------------- At Salesforce, we believe that talent is equally distributed, but opportunity is not. Our Futureforce team is on a mission to change that, building pathways for the next generation of talent through world\-class internship programmes, university partnerships, and global recruiting events. If you're passionate about people, programmes, and making things happen, we'd love to work with you. **The Opportunity** ------------------- We're looking for a driven and organised contractor to join our Futureforce EMEA team in Dublin. Reporting directly to the Senior Manager for Programs \& Events, Futureforce International, you'll take ownership of a varied and exciting portfolio, from managing internship cohorts to delivering high\-profile recruiting events across Europe. This is a hands\-on, high\-impact role for someone who thrives on variety, takes pride in flawless execution, and loves working in a fast\-moving, people\-first environment. **What You'll Be Working On** ----------------------------- ### **Internship Programme Management** You'll play a central role in delivering our EMEA internship programme, supporting multiple cohorts across the year. From onboarding logistics to manager engagement and ongoing activations, you'll help ensure every intern has an outstanding experience. ### **Events \& Office Tours** You'll support the planning and delivery of a calendar of recruiting events and campus activations across EMEA, including flagship conferences and office visits. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. Posting Statement Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: GTM Data & Performance Analyst
description: Created in 2003, Smartbox is the French and European number 1 in experience gifts, with 6\.5 million boxes sold each year, around €350 million annual turnover, in a gift box market, offering the widest range of experience gifts. For more than 20 years, we have been developing thousands of experiences to offer and an equal number of unforgettable moments to live. From the most iconic to the most unique, Smartbox's mission has always been to enable everyone to gift their loved ones the opportunity to live unique experiences covering the themes of stay, adventures, well\-being and gastronomy. In the short future, we want to be the point of reference when it comes to inspire people to live experiences. We are at a tipping point in terms of innovation on our market. The group employs 800 collaborators based across Europe. Smartbox gift boxes are sold in 10 European countries: France, Italy, Spain, Belgium, Switzerland, the Netherlands, Denmark, Sweden, Portugal and Germany. Pioneer in the sector and number 1 in most of these countries, significantly ahead of its competitors, the group relies on a network of approximately 150 partner retailers throughout Europe, i.e. 18,000 major points of, and distributes its products as well as to the websites of the various brands of the group and with online distributors such as Amazon. One of the keys to the group's success is to have built a network of 30,000 partners in Europe. The Group has been owned since 2013 by Otium Capital, which has been able to support more than 50 start\-ups and innovative companies thanks to the profits generated by the Smartbox Group. More than just a box, Smartbox has been one of the biggest success story in the industry in the last 20 years. **About Your Role:** As a GTM Data \& Performance Analyst, you will support the GTM Strategy and Sales teams by producing reliable analyses, reports and dashboards that help monitor partner performance and commercial activity. This is an execution‑focused analytical role. You will work on clearly defined topics and priorities, contribute to recurring reporting and analyses, and help ensure that commercial teams have access to accurate and consistent data to support decision‑making. You will collaborate closely with GTM Strategy, RevOps and Sales, and interact regularly with Product, Tech and Finance to provide data inputs and insights. **Key Responsibilities** **Data Analysis \& Partner Performance:** * Produce recurring reports and analyses on partner performance across predefined dimensions such as partner tier, product category and geography. * Monitor key performance indicators (e.g. revenue contribution, availability, catalog quality, NPS) and highlight notable variations or data issues. * Prepare structured analyses to support reviews of partner performance, under the guidance of GTM Strategy. * Support quarterly and monthly business reviews by preparing data extracts, charts and summaries. * Track sales performance against targets for Account Managers and BDMs, and prepare standard reports for review by GTM leadership. **Pricing \& Commercial Analysis Support:** * Support pricing and margin analyses by preparing data inputs, simulations and comparison reports. * Assist in modelling the commercial impact of predefined pricing or incentive scenarios. * Help monitor pricing consistency across markets and categories and flag anomalies to relevant stakeholders. * Provide analytical support for new commercial initiatives (e.g. new offer types or partner models), based on clearly defined scopes. **Dashboards \& Reporting:** * Maintain and update existing GTM dashboards used by GTM Strategy, Sales teams and country stakeholders. * Ensure data accuracy and consistency across reports and dashboards. * Implement dashboard changes and updates based on documented business requirements. * Support the adoption of dashboards by commercial teams by helping explain metrics and reports. **Marketplace \& Product Data Support:** * Develop a good understanding of Smartbox’s marketplace and catalog performance metrics. * Track predefined catalog health indicators and prepare reports for Product and Operations teams. * Provide data and analysis to support cross‑functional discussions on marketplace performance when requested. * Assist in compiling competitor and market data to support GTM analyses. **Requirements for the Role:** * 2–4 years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence or a related analytical role. * Good working knowledge of SQL and at least one BI or data visualisation tool (e.g. Tableau, Looker, Metabase, Power BI). * Basic understanding of commercial, sales or marketplace environments. * Ability to work with structured datasets and follow established analytical frameworks. * Comfortable collaborating with multiple teams (Sales, GTM Strategy, RevOps, Product, Finance). * Strong attention to detail and data quality. **Skills:** * Ability to execute data analyses accurately and on time. * Structured and methodical approach to problem solving. * Ability to summarise data and findings clearly. * Good communication skills, with the ability to explain metrics and reports to non‑technical stakeholders. * Willingness to learn and develop analytical and commercial skills. * Adaptability in a dynamic business environment. **What we value:** * **Reliability**: you deliver high‑quality analyses that teams can trust. * **Curiosity**: you are eager to understand the business and how data supports it. * **Collaboration**: you work effectively with different teams and functions. * **Growth mindset**: you are motivated to develop your skills and take on increasing responsibility over time.

title: General Accountant
description: Want to leverage your Accounting experience in an innovative, fast\-growing company in the Computer Networking industry? If yes, this role might be for you: **Domotz** is looking for a **General Accountant.** We are now looking for a committed, self\-motivated General Accountant with a can\-do attitude, eager to take on new challenges in this field. Our ideal candidate is punctual and excited to grow, striving for excellence. ***Role Description and Responsibilities:*** This is a full\-time role with **a hybrid remote working solution** . The General Accountant will be responsible for: * Manage the account receivable and billing processes (Amazon, Stripe \& Google Play) for 2 entities of Domotz Group. * Manage offline orders requested by Sales department. * Follow up with customers with outstanding payments. * Review and post deferred revenue and indirect tax entries (Sales tax, VAT, OSS \& VIES). * Support the Senior Accountant to prepare and review the working trial balance. * Support account payable activities. * Manage intercompany transactions. * Support the Senior Accountant in ad hoc requests. **Qualifications** * Proficient in financial reporting and maintaining financial records * Familiarity with tax regulations and tax filings for Europe (Ireland \& UK) and US. * Good understanding of Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP). * Proficiency in accounting software and tools such as Xero (preferable) and Microsoft Excel (strong knowledge required). * Passion for AI with a proactive mindset toward automating and optimizing processes. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to work independently and remotely. * Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance or related field (ACCA). * Italian language proficiency will be an advantage. ***Why Join The Finance Team At Domotz?*** * Permanent position and Health Insurance after probation period * Competitive compensation commensurate with experience. * Hybrid working, with two days per week in our office in Dublin. * Opportunity for a career growth in a very innovative and international company. * Attention to people's wellbeing and work\-life balance. ***About Domotz*** Domotz is the leading emerging network monitoring software that provides unparalleled visibility of digital assets and data flows. We have offices in USA, Italy, Ireland, and customers in 190 countries. Our award\-winning platform offers powerful network monitoring and management software for MSPs, System Integrators, Enterprises, IT Security Professionals, and Business Owners. Our mission is to empower our users with mission\-critical knowledge and actionable insights from their digital networks, helping them in remote managing and solving even the most challenging Network and IT issues in an efficient, secure and cost\-effective way. *If you have strong Excel knowledge, a passion for Accounting and Finance field, and want to be part of an exciting journey in a fast\-growing company, then we want to hear from you!*

title: General Operative
description: **Company:** Dawn Meats Kildare **Job Title:** General Operative. **Reporting To:** Production Manager. **Position type:** Permanent **Role Summary:** Dawn Meats Kildare have opportunities for General Operatives to join our production team. We are seeking reliable and hardworking General Operatives to join an industry leader that provides opportunities for training and progression to the right candidates. **Responsibilities** **The successful candidate will be based at our primary production facility in Kildare town and will be responsible for:** * Role based primarily in the Boning Hall; * Successful candidates will work as part of a team in the factory, packing meat cuts and trims to customer specification on a paced production line to fulfil production targets on a daily and weekly basis; * To assist on the production area \& provide support to the production team; * Work in all areas of the factory; * Keep good housekeeping standards in all areas; * Keep up to date knowledge of all products; * Keep up to date with all training to ensure you are trained to complete your day to day role; * Follow work instructions in all areas; * Ensure you comply with Health \& Safety regulations; * Ensure all data to be recorded is accurate and up to date; * Ensure quality guidelines are followed for all areas; * Undertake flexible working hours to meet deadline. **Qualifications** **Your role will see you tackle a wide range of business challenges in a busy production environment.** **The ideal Candidate will have or demonstrate:** * Entry level, semi\-skilled \& advanced positions available; * An ability to work to deadlines; * An ability to work on own initiative; * Interested in future development \& progression. INDLP

title: Geomatics Associate
description: At Dalcour Maclaren, we're key players in the Utilities and Infrastructure industry, bringing the UK and Ireland closer to Net Zero every day. Our land, environmental and geospatial experts work together to unlock the consents needed by our clients to deliver key projects. Our services include land access and assembly, agricultural liaison, planning, environmental impact assessments and geospatial mapping. Join us, and play your part in helping us to achieve our vision; to be the professional services team of choice, leading the Utilities and Infrastructure industry to a sustainable future. Due to continued and strong anticipated growth in our workstreams in the Republic of Ireland (RoI), the Geomatics team have an opportunity for an Associate to join our team in Dublin. The successful candidate will work closely with the GIS and Land Referencing Service Delivery Leads as well as the Land and Property team delivering on infrastructure projects across Ireland. The candidate will also support the Ireland Growth lead with business development activities including tendering and client engagement. The role will involve the management of all spatial and property diligence deliverables associated with the delivery of infrastructure projects at all stages from site/route appraisal through to construction. It will also include the provision of strategic advice to clients in relation to property diligence matters and the requirements associated. **The successful candidate will:** * **(Be required to work from our Dublin office a minimum of three days per week due to the leadership and management responsibilities associated with this role).** * Have experience of working with property records and information systems including Tailte Éireann along with associated knowledge of relevant infrastructure consenting regimes. * Have knowledge and experience of land rights and the methods by which they can be procured either through negotiation or through statutory means including CPOs (Ordú Ceannach Éigeantach) or Statutory Property Notices (Wayleaves/Easements). * Have practical GIS experience and ideally within the property and/or the utility/energy sector. * Have strong knowledge of ESRI ArcGIS software for spatial data capture, management and analysis. * Have project management experience including creation and management of project programmes, forecasts and budgets as well as the ability to provide reports detailing performance against agreed key performance indicators. * Be commercially aware. * Have great leadership qualities. * Be highly driven with strong communication skills. * Have a degree or master’s in GIS or a closely related subject such as Geography, Surveying, Land Management with a large GIS component. **As a Geomatics Associate you will:** * Be a member of the Geomatics Leadership team, working collaboratively with the wider Land Referencing and GIS teams to deliver an excellent innovative service and build strong client relationships. * To influence the direction of the Geomatics team in the Republic of Ireland and to deliver an excellent innovative service. * Build strong client relationships. * Report progress against Dalcour Maclaren’s Republic of Ireland business plan centring on people, service and sustainable growth, innovations, business development/opportunities, good news stories, lessons learnt and own and escalate risks. * Lead in identifying key data sources, engaging with suppliers to licence, obtain and set up the required environment and process to gain best value of them. * Manage projects and requests, ensuring requirements and objectives are met, building positive relationships and taking ownership of the standard of work. * Be able to accurately assess the time requirements of a request and be confident in advising clients and surveyors. * Fully understand all client requirements to provide guidance to junior team members and quality assure deliverables to ensure standards are met at all times. * Support the development of more junior members of staff by line managing, providing feedback and conducting their progress reviews. **Benefits:** * Annual bonus scheme * 25 days annual leave plus 1 day to celebrate your birthday * Vitality Health Membership (including Private healthcare, subsidised gym membership and other discounts) * Flexible working through our ‘Flexible Advantage’ supporting working families \& your work/life balance * Pension Scheme via The Peoples Pension * Life insurance (4x Base Salary) * Free on\-site parking in non\-city centre offices * Cycle to work scheme * 26 Weeks paid Maternity Leave * 4 Weeks paid Paternity Leave * BHN Extras – provides discounted gift cards for hundreds of popular retailers * Electric Vehicle Salary Sacrifice Scheme (provided by Octopus Electric Vehicles) effective at 1 years’ service * Sabbatical Policy effective at 3 years’ service * Professional development – paid study leave * Military service paid leave (7\.5 days per year) * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) plus wider Mental Health \& Wellbeing support * 2 x Company\-wide events every year, in addition to multiple office socials throughout the year *Dalcour Maclaren is an equal opportunities employer.*

title: Graduate Boarding Assistant
description: Clongowes Wood College is one of Ireland’s oldest and most successful boarding schools with an enrolment of c.430\. The College is committed to the provision of the highest quality Catholic education in the Jesuit tradition. We are currently recruiting for a **Graduate Boarding Assistnat** A Graduate Boarding Assistant works alongside the Prefecting (Boarding ) team, including other recent graduates, in all aspects of their duties. Prefects in Clongowes are Housemasters or Housemistresses who are responsible for the day to day pastoral care of the students. The role will require a flexible approach to working but typical duties will include: * Help establish a home\-like, family atmosphere embodying the College’s Jesuit values, and facilitate possibilities for the boys to grow in their faith. * Supervising and leading students through their day to day routines. * Extracurricular activities, in particular sport, play an important part of life in Clongowes. The Prefecting team are involved in helping to coach sports teams. Similarly, Graduate Boarding Assistants are also expected to facilitate boys in their extracurricular and sporting interests. * Assist the Prefecting team with practical tasks in the dormitory and around the College. Duties include **two overnight shifts per week plus sporting commitments.** Graduate Prefects have accommodation within the College for the year, including full board, use of the College Library and sports facilities. The position will suit a graduate student who is considering a future in education and it is possible that the position can be combined with a teaching placement in Clongowes as part of a PME. The position would also suit someone who is seeking employment alongside other post\-graduate studies or has a passion for sport. The ideal candidate will have * An interest in working with young people, ideally within a teaching or coaching capacity. * Possess a high level of maturity with the ability to build strong relationships with boys, parents or colleagues. * Supportive of our Jesuit ethos. * Demonstrate relevant skills, experience and qualifications in an area of expertise, and genuinely enjoy sharing those skills for the development of young people. * Naturally possess a positive mind\-set. * Confident and persuasive written and verbal communicator. * Collaborative and flexible approach \& comfortable working as part of a team. * Calm under pressure; employ tact and diplomacy in difficult or sensitive situations. * Have a commitment to safeguarding and the welfare of young people. * Demonstrate a high level of emotional intelligence. * A good sense of humour and a sense of proportion. **Term** The post is fixed term from **late August 2026 to the end of June 2027\.** **Residency requirement** Successful candidates must be entitled to work and reside in Ireland. **Accommodation** Single accommodation onsite is included. **Safeguarding** The interview process will explore issues relating to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children. The appointment will be subject to the satisfactory outcome of Garda Vetting. Pay: €1,200\.00 per month Benefits: * Food allowance * Gym membership * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Business Solutions Developer (Multimedia)
description: We are seeking to recruit a Graduate Business Solutions Developer (Multimedia) to join our creative and technical team in producing engaging, interactive digital learning content for our extensive global client base. This role offers an exciting opportunity to contribute to the design and development of high\-quality front\-end multimedia solutions that support over a million active corporate learners each year. The position is based in our Dublin office and provides an excellent foundation for a graduate looking to begin their career within a leading technology\-driven learning organisation. The ideal candidate will be a creative, curious, and motivated graduate with an understanding of front\-end and multimedia design principles and a passion for crafting visually compelling digital experiences. They should bring problem solving abilities, an eagerness to learn new tools and techniques, and a collaborative mindset. Attention to detail, a proactive approach, and effective communication skills will be essential in delivering polished, user\-centred solutions while continuously expanding their technical and creative capabilities. The successful candidate will work closely with an international team of instructional designers, software engineers, project managers, and client\-facing teams to deliver innovative multimedia content and interactive learning experiences. They will play a key role in transforming complex ideas into intuitive, engaging digital assets that support our clients’ goals in learning, performance improvement, and digital transformation worldwide. **This role involves:** •Transforming learning designs into interactive digital media optimised for phones, tablets, web browsers, and emerging AI\-enabled interfaces. •Using technologies such as Angular, Node.js, and Python as part this work to output intuitive user interfaces and user experiences in HTML, JavaScript, and CSS. •Influencing and advising cross\-functional teams, sharing ideas and contributing to collaborative project outcomes. •Creating high\-quality digital learning content using industry\-standard tools like Articulate Rise and Articulate Storyline. •Diagnosing, fixing, and troubleshooting issues across both traditional and AI\-enabled experiences to ensure smooth, reliable learner journeys. •Preparing and working with content and structured data for use with automation and AI systems such as Retrieval\-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. •Ensuring the accurate and effective implementation of digital media, maintaining consistently high standards of quality. •Testing and validating digital content across multiple platforms and Learning Management Systems, ensuring flawless performance. •Keeping up to date with fast\-evolving eLearning, multimedia, and online technology trends, bringing fresh ideas to the team. **Additional Skills \& Attributes:** •Curiosity and enthusiasm for new tools, technologies, modern development practices, conversational interfaces, AI\-assisted user experiences, and how design choices influence AI behaviour. •Willingness to continually learn and improve. •Strong attention to detail supported by an analytical mindset. •Effective communication skills and a collaborative working style. •Strong logical thinking and problem\-solving ability. •Appreciation of and interest in good design, both technically and visually. •Ability to think clearly about structure, consistency, and constraints when working with digital content. **Minimum requirements:** •Bachelor’s or master’s degree in a field related to multimedia, computer science, web development, or programming. •Exposure through academic or personal projects to data\-driven applications, conversational interfaces, or AI\-assisted tools is an advantage, but not required. •A good understanding of interface design, digital graphics or digital media, video, and audio. Intuition is an Equal Opportunities Employer

title: Graduate Health and Safety Advisors 2026
description: **Position:** Graduate Health and Safety Advisors 2026 **Location** : Dublin, Kildare, Cork, Limerick, Gavle, Copenhagen, Groningen, Charleroiand UK **Start date:** We operate on a rolling recruitment basis. Please advise your availability to start in your application form. You can discuss this further with your recruiter upon application. **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Job Description** As a Graduate Health and Safety Advisor, you will play a pivotal role in our project implementation and delivery processes. You will work closely with a multidisciplinary team of engineers and industry experts to safely deliver the completion of projects. Your responsibilities will include managing and maintaining the company’s Safety Management System and CMS System. **Your Responsibilities will include:** * Providing general support and assistance to the JEG Site EHS Team * Assisting with site administration and weekly reports * Conducting weekly site audits * Assisting with Health and Safety Inductions * Maintaining training matrix and advise management of training requirements * Carrying out site inspections and audits with the EHS Team * Collecting and collating relevant information for inclusion in the Safety Management System **Skills And Requirements** To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: * Relevant 3rd Level Occupational Safety \& Health qualification essential * Proficiency in using Microsoft Office required * Excellent organisational and planning skills * Demonstrated ability to communicate and work well in a team environment **Benefits** Be part of our Graduate Programme and contribute to meaningful projects that drive innovation and sustainability across multiple industries. We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note. We are currently not accepting application from agencies at this time.

title: Graduate Quantity Surveyors 2026
description: **Graduate Quantity Surveyor 2026** **Position** : Graduate Quantity Surveyor 2026 **Location** : Dublin, Kildare, Cork, Limerick, Gavle, Copenhagen, Groningen, Charleroi, Hillerod **Start date:** We operate on a rolling recruitment basis.
