Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Job Description** * Work closely with the Senior Quantity Surveyor as well as a multidisciplinary team of engineers and industry experts to deliver cutting\-edge projects. * Assist the team with monthly progress applications. * Generate and substantiate variations and contracts administration for all projects. * Support the team with reporting on a weekly and monthly basis. * Complete site progress walks. * Assist in production/coordination of weekly construction reports. This role will provide exposure to a wide range of expertise relating to pharmaceutical, life sciences, data centre, construction and other sectors. The successful candidate will be supported in developing their knowledge and skills as their responsibilities grow and evolve to achieve Chartered Status. **Requirements** To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: * Degree Qualification in Quantity Surveying/Construction/Engineering. * Valid Safepass / Manual handling certificates required for successful applicants * Experience in construction industry is advantageous, demonstrating your understanding of industry best practices and technologies. * 0\-1 years’ post qualification experience in an M\&E contracting environment. * Strong team player with excellent interpersonal skills, able to collaborate effectively with colleagues, clients, and external partners. * Excellent organisational and planning skills. * Proficiency in MS Office systems required. * Demonstrated appetite for continuous learning and personal development. **Graduate Development Programme** As part of our commitment to your growth and career development, you will be enrolled in our comprehensive 2\-year Graduate Development Programme. This programme includes experiential learning, mentoring, professional development courses, and in\-house training on key skills such as technical writing, presentation, and time management. You will benefit from peer to peer learning and receive support for obtaining professional certifications. This structured approach ensures you gain the necessary skills and experience to progress in your career. **Benefits** Be part of our Graduate Programme and contribute to meaningful projects that drive innovation and sustainability across multiple industries. We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note, we are currently not accepting applications from agencies for this role.

title: Graduate Sales Executive (Benelux Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in Dutch and English * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Graduate Sales and Application Engineer
description: **Launch Your Engineering Career with a Global Industry Leader** Are you a recent engineering graduate looking to combine technical problem\-solving with a future career in commercial engineering and sales? SPX Cooling Technologies is offering an exciting opportunity for a Graduate Sales \& Applications Engineer to join our Cooling Towers \& Accessories team in Ireland. This newly created role is designed to develop the next generation of technical and commercial talent within our business. You will gain hands\-on experience working on real HVAC and process cooling projects, learning how to review technical specifications, support quotations, and help deliver engineered solutions for customers across multiple industries. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who enjoys engineering detail, wants exposure to commercial projects, and is eager to develop toward a future customer\-facing engineering or sales role. **Why Join SPX Cooling Technologies?** * Join a global leader in cooling tower and thermal management solutions * Structured graduate development and mentoring from experienced engineers * Gain exposure to HVAC, process cooling, and industrial applications * Learn both the technical and commercial sides of engineering projects * Clear long\-term progression into applications engineering, technical sales, and leadership roles * Supportive team environment with ongoing training and development **The Role** As a Graduate Sales \& Applications Engineer, you will support the technical and commercial teams by assisting with product selection, proposal preparation, and engineering analysis. You will work closely with experienced colleagues to learn SPX systems, engineering tools, and customer requirements while developing your technical confidence and commercial awareness. **Key Responsibilities** **Technical Support** * Assist with reviewing RFQs, specifications, and technical drawings * Learn to use SPX selection software (QTC) to configure suitable solutions * Support the preparation of technical proposals and submittal documents * Help ensure product selections meet customer and project requirements * Work with internal engineering and sales teams on project coordination **Commercial \& Sales Exposure** * Support quotation and proposal development * Attend customer meetings and presentations alongside senior team members * Develop understanding of pricing, project delivery, and market competitors * Assist with maintaining project pipeline and sales data * Build commercial awareness within a technical engineering environment **What We’re Looking For** * Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Energy Engineering, Building Services Engineering, or similar * Strong interest in HVAC, cooling systems, thermodynamics, or industrial engineering * Good analytical and problem\-solving skills * Ability to interpret technical information and engineering drawings * Strong communication skills and willingness to learn * Motivated individual looking to develop a long\-term engineering career **Development \& Career Progression** This role has been designed as a long\-term development opportunity. You will receive structured support and mentoring with a clear pathway into: * Applications Engineering * Technical Sales * Key Account Management * Commercial Leadership If you are looking for a graduate engineering role that offers both technical depth and future commercial progression, we would love to hear from you. Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €35,000\.00\-€42,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Sales and Marketing Executive
description: Aline Office Furniture supplies new, used and rental office furniture solutions to customers across Ireland. From complete office fit outs to flexible rental options, we help our customers create professional and practical workspaces. We offer a fast\-paced and hands\-on environment where team members have the opportunity to take ownership and contribute ideas from day one. We are looking for an ambitious and creative individual to grow with the company. **The Role:** This is an exciting opportunity for a recent graduate to gain hands\-on experience in both sales and digital marketing within a fast\-paced commercial environment. The role will involve managing social media channels, supporting online advertising campaigns, analysing marketing performance, and assisting with sales and customer engagement. The successful candidate will play a key role in promoting our new, used, and rental office furniture offerings while helping to generate leads and grow brand awareness. **Key Responsibilities:** · Managing and scheduling content across social media channels. · Creating content such as product photos, short videos and promotional posts. · Keeping the website updated with product listings and completed office fit\-out projects. · Reviewing website and campaign analytics using Google Analytics. · Running Google Ads campaigns and monitoring ad performance. · Researching competitors, industry trends, and marketing ideas. · Following up on sales leads and customer enquiries by phone or email in a timely, professional, and effective manner. · Assisting with quotations and proposals for customers. · Maintaining customer records and updating CRM systems. · Supporting showroom presentation and product displays. · Coordinating with suppliers and internal sales staff. · Helping organise promotions, campaigns, and seasonal offers. · Assisting with email marketing campaigns and newsletters. · General administrative support for the sales and marketing team. **Candidate Profile:** · Recent graduate in Marketing, Business, Digital Media, Communications, or a related field. · Strong interest in sales, digital marketing, and business development. · Familiarity with social media platforms and content creation tools. · Excellent communication and organisational skills. · Creative, proactive, and commercially minded. · Strong attention to detail with the ability to multitask. · Ability to work independently and as part of a growing team. · Familiarity with Google Ads, Google Analytics, and digital marketing tools is preferred. **What We Offer:** · Competitive salary and benefits package. · 3pm finish on Fridays. · Hands\-on experience across sales and digital marketing from day one. · Training, mentorship, and support with professional development. · Supportive and collaborative team environment. · Opportunity to develop and own marketing campaigns and digital channels. Job Type: Full\-time, permanent Location: Dublin based, in person Pay: €31,000\.00\-€31,200\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Guest Services Host - Catering Assistant
description: We are seeking a dedicated candidate to assist our Rapport and Restaurant Associates teams in their daily operations. This role is designed to provide a supportive environment that fosters professional growth and skill development. The candidate will engage within various tasks including organizing workflow, communicating among the team members and enhancing overall efficiency of service delivery. Candidate should possess interpersonal skills, willingness to learn and genuine interest in helping others. This temporary role offers valuable experience in collaborative setting, promoting both personal and professional developments. You’ll be joining a collaborative EMEA team of 24 and continuing to expand. Despite being spread across the region, the team works closely together and is eager to welcome a new member. In this role, you’ll be a vital presence on\-site, fostering a positive, engaged workplace community, supporting employee satisfaction, and addressing any day\-to\-day challenges that arise. ***Type of contract:*** Part\-time, Fixed term ***Hours:*** 6 per week (Tuesdays \& Wednesdays: 3\-hours per day) **What will you get?** * **Financial Freedom:** Enjoy exclusive high\-street discounts and wellbeing benefits just one click away with Unlock, our benefits platform! * **Transportation Benefits:** We offer tax\-saver tickets on public transport in partnership with Travel Hub and/or Bike to Work Scheme (providing you with sustainable transport modes, incorporating physical activity into your everyday commuting). * **Secure Your Future:** Benefit from a contributory pension scheme using MyFutureFund, helping you save for retirement automatically. * **Health Matters:** In addition to our wellness programmes, we offer eye examination vouchers for those requiring a Visual Display Unit (VDU/DSE) test. * **Thrive Personally \& Professionally:** Unlock endless learning and development opportunities to elevate your career! * **Celebrate Excellence:** Shine a spotlight on outstanding peers with WOW Awards for their exceptional contributions. * **Give Back to Community:** Take one paid day off annually to support a cause you’re passionate about! **Main responsibilities** * Assisting with set up of client meeting rooms i.e. ensuring all note pads, pens etc. are always stocked. * Checking the rooms before and after each meeting. * Assisting with escorting visitors to client meeting rooms. * Assisting with preparations for events\- i.e. preparing and organizing name badges. * Assisting with Out of hours events\- escorting, guiding etc. * Assisting with Cloakroom management. * Supporting the Hospitality team in ensuring the refreshment area at the reception is well always kept neat and tidy. * Keeping the Grab \& Go fridge stocked up and replenishing any items needed in our Café(s) i.e. coffee cups, spoons etc. * Assisting with set up and clearance of Client meeting spaces pre and post meetings with food and beverage elements. **The ideal candidate will** * Share our Rapport values: ***Create Rapport, Be Brilliant, Innovative Spirit, Walk Tall and Stronger Together.*** * Ideally, have at least one year of experience in a similar role within a fast\-paced and dynamic establishment (e.g., five\-star hotel or corporate environment). * Be proactive, reliable, and flexible. * Continuously learn and provide support to immediate and the wider teams. * Adhere to company policies and procedures. * Look for opportunities for improvement and enhancement of our services. * Be effective communicator and team player. **About us** Rapport Guest Services is a multi\-award\-winning company, delivering front\- and back\-of\-house guest services for leading organisations across the UK, Ireland, and globally. As part of Compass Group UK\&I, we combine people\-first culture with exceptional service standards. We are proud of our commitment to inclusion, wellbeing, and creating opportunities for everyone to thrive. We are also a Disability Confident Employer, offering meaningful roles for individuals with additional needs. Find out more about us and our values at www.rapportservice.com. *Rapport job adverts always gain a high response rate, but we do review all applications. Please check your inbox and your junk/spam folder for our response to your application. Due to high application volumes, we recommend that you do not apply for more than one role at any time. Please ensure you apply for the role that best matches your experience.*

title: Guidewire Developer
description: My client, a leading multinational based in Dublin are looking to hire multiple Guidewire Developers on a contract basis. The successful candidates will play a key role in delivering high\-quality platform solutions, working across Guidewire applications to design, develop and enhance systems that support critical business ops. The Role: * Design, develop, and maintain solutions across Guidewire applications. * Collaborate with business analysts and stakeholders to translate requirements into technical deliverables. * Configure and customise Guidewire using Gosu, PCF, and business rules. * Develop integrations between Guidewire and external systems using APIs and messaging frameworks * Support system enhancements, defect resolution, and ongoing platform optimisation. * Participate in code reviews, design discussions, and agile ceremonies. * Ensure adherence to development best practices, coding standards and secure coding principles. * Contribute to testing activities including unit testing, integration testing, and deployment support. * Work within Agile/DevOps environments to ensure continuous delivery and improvement For more info or to apply, contact Michael O'Brien on michael.obrien@db\-recruitment.ie

title: HR Generalist (€55K–€60K DOE)
description: **Company Description** At Securitas Security Services Ireland, the human resources (HR) team is focused on what people need and how to provide it. For a people person who wants to begin a career in the exciting world of HR, this is the best place to dive in. We are searching for a qualified and resourceful Human Resources Generalist to support our department in ensuring smooth and efficient business operations. The Human Resources Generalist will have both administrative and strategic responsibilities and will help with important various important functions within the H.R. department. We understand that our business thrives when our employees thrive, and it begins with hiring the right Human Resources Generalist. **Are you ready to join a fantastic team!** Dublin 15 Monday to Friday, 9\.00am \- 5\.30pm (Hybrid Role) €55,000 \- €60,000 DOE **Why join us?** * Be part of a company that lives by its values: Integrity, Vigilance, and Helpfulness * Career development opportunities and industry\-recognised training * A supportive team environment * Competitive salary and benefits package * Make a meaningful impact every day * Join Securitas and help us “See a Different World.” Apply now and become a key player in delivering safety and peace of mind. * Job Description * Assist in developing and executing personnel procedures and policies, and provide guidance and interpretation for business operations. * Participate in development of HR objectives and systems, including metrics, queries, and ongoing reports for company requirements. * Support with case file coordination relevant to WRC hearings, within agreed timeframes. * Assist in administering benefits, compensation, and employee performance programs. * Suggest new procedures and policies for improving employee experience as well as the efficiency of HR department and company. * Ensure compliance with local and national regulations and applicable employment laws and update policies and procedures when necessary. * Prepare paperwork and schedules for smooth new\-hire onboarding process, coordinating with cross\-functional departments to deliver an exceptional first\-day experience. * Support all administrative tasks for onboarding, new\-hire orientations, and exit interviews, including data entry in human resources information systems (HRIS) and audits for accuracy and compliance. * Provide a dedicated and effective HR advisory service to employees that covers absence and health issues, conduct and capability, grievances, organizational change, and all other employee\-relations matters. * Be the primary backup for payroll processing, including biweekly and monthly updates to employee files, bonus/incentive pay, vacation/sick pay, expense reimbursements, hourly\-employee validations, and benefits changes. * Assist in the communication, interpretation, and upkeep of employee handbook, employee directory, and organizational chart, and contribute to policy development. * Managing employee complaints, managing grievance procedures, and supporting counselling with other stakeholders. * Investigating and resolving complex or critical employee relations issues in a timely and effective manner. * Manage the dedicated HR email account. * Any other ad hoc duties as requested by your Line Manager. **Qualifications** * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, ethics, and cultural awareness * Aptitude for problem\-solving and thorough knowledge of HR procedures and policies * Advanced knowledge of HRIS and ability to learn new technical systems, when necessary * Detail\-oriented with a commitment to HR and employee relations * Collaborative approach with a focus on professional development. * Ability to determine courses of action based upon detailed written instructions. * Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in human resources, business, or related field * Proven success working in a HR department * Resourceful mindset and strong attention to detail * Knowledge of Microsoft software * Knowledge of national laws and regulations related to employment law * Excellent organisational, communication, and interpersonal abilities * CIPD Qualification * Must have 3\-5 years experience in Employee Relations * Demonstrated experience in representing and attending the WRC on behalf of the organisation. **Additional Information** At Securitas, we believe in rewarding our people for their hard work. We offer a range of great benefits designed to support our people and their families. These comprehensive benefits include discounted health care scheme, company pension, bike to work scheme, access to hundreds of discounts from well\-known brands and retailers, access to hundreds of high\-quality online education courses from some of the world’s leading learning institutions, ways to save for the future, and other resources to improve health and well\-being. **Benefits** * Substantial Maternity Leave top up payment * 24 hour EAP (Employee Assistance Programme) free confidential counselling and advice supporting our employees in a number of areas. * Employer Contribution Pension Scheme from the day you join the Company * Progression, training and development opportunities * Christmas Savings Scheme * Discounted Private Healthcare Scheme (Cash back on everyday medical costs, such as dental check\-ups, optical, GP and Emergency Department visits.) * Internal recognition schemes * Bicycle to work scheme from the day you join the Company (Save up to 52% on a bike and accessories) Securitas is an equal opportunities Employer P.S.A. Licence Number : 00352

title: Helpdesk Engineer
description: **IT Helpdesk Engineer** Ergo are seeking an IT Helpdesk Engineer for a permanent role in West Dublin. As an IT Helpdesk Engineer you will provide 1st and 2nd level technical support to an M365 user group both remotely and on\-site. **Requirements** \- 2\+ years of experience in an L1/L2 IT Support or Service Desk environment \- Experience supporting Windows 10/11 \- Strong understanding of Microsoft 365 applications \- Exposure to virtual desktop environments (e.g., Azure Virtual Desktop, Citrix, VMware Horizon) \- Strong communication skills and a customer\-focused approach \- Microsoft certifications are beneficial but not essential \- Own transport an advantage **Responsibilities** \- Support end users across Windows, MacOS, iOS, and virtual desktop environments \- Troubleshoot issues with desktops, laptops, printers, mobile devices, and Microsoft 365 applications \- Assist with Microsoft 365 administration (Teams, OneDrive, SharePoint) \- Perform basic Active Directory tasks such as user creation, password resets, and group management \- Provide Deskside onsite support \- Assist senior engineers with IT project implementation and deployments Apply today for more information.

title: IT & Systems Administrator
description: Fine Gael is seeking an IT \& Systems Administrator to manage and support the organisation’s IT systems and digital infrastructure. This role is responsible for ensuring the smooth day\-to\-day operation of IT services, supporting staff, and maintaining secure and efficient systems. The role is based in Dublin 2 and has a strong focus on Microsoft 365 and cloud\-based tools. There is also an opportunity to support improvements in how data is structured and used across systems, including dashboard and reporting work where appropriate. It is a role that will be of particular interest to individuals who are interested in and curious about how technology can be used to support political activity. The role offers a competitive salary, career progression opportunities and generous annual leave allowance. **Main Duties \& Responsibilities** *IT Systems \& Support* * Manage and administer Microsoft 365 (users, licenses, Teams, SharePoint, Exchange). * Provide day\-to\-day IT support to staff (hardware, software and access issues). * Manage onboarding and offboarding of users (accounts, permissions and devices). * Maintain and support laptops, printers and other equipment. * Troubleshoot system issues and liaise with external IT providers were required * Support the management and maintenance of the Fine Gael website. * Provide IT support for Party events, including Ard Fheis, conferences and online meetings, ensuring systems and technology run smoothly. *Security \& Compliance* * Maintain IT security including user access controls, MFA and device security. * Support compliance with GDPR and internal data protection policies. * Maintain backups and support disaster recovery processes. * Ensure appropriate controls are in place for handling sensitive data. *Systems \& Process Improvement* * Support the development and improvement of internal systems and workflows. * Assist in automating processes where possible. * Work with teams to ensure systems are used effectively. * Stay up to date with emerging technologies and identify opportunities to improve systems, efficiency and user experience across the organisation. *Data \& Reporting Support* * Support the development and maintenance of dashboards (e.g. Power BI). * Assist in structuring data across systems to enable reporting. * Work with teams to improve data quality and consistency. * Provide basic reporting support where required. **Skills** * Strong experience administering Microsoft 365\. * Experience providing end\-user IT support. * Good understanding of IT security and data protection. * Experience working with cloud\-based systems. * Ability to manage external vendors and service providers. * Strong problem\-solving skills and ability to work independently. * Experience with Power BI and Power Automate (or similar tools), along with an understanding of data structures and reporting. **Note:** * Full Time, Fixed term 2\-year Contract . * Closing Date, 5pm Friday 29th May 2026

title: IT Support Engineer
description: **Job Description** Nostra is a leading Managed Service Provider (MSP) in Ireland, dedicated to delivering advanced IT solutions that enable businesses to build reliable, secure, and scalable IT foundations. With a commitment to continuous improvement, we serve a diverse range of industries, providing exceptional services that empower our clients to focus on their core business goals. The ideal candidate would have experience in cloud infrastructure including endpoint management, Networking, Server Administration, Azure, and Office 365 as well as a solid understanding of on\-premise technology including networking. This role would require a service\-oriented attitude, excellent verbal and written skills and a high focus on the quality of work. Nostra has offices in Dublin (HQ), Cork, Galway and Belfast. **Responsibilities:** * Manage the IT Support queue. * Provide support on client sites as per business requirements. * Take ownership of IT issues and work with the team (and SMEs across the business as required), to resolve these issues. * Document processes and procedures to increase the efficiency of the service desk team. * Support M365 environment and perform server and laptop setups and repairs. * Install, configure and maintain user software and communications equipment. * Manage and communicate with third party vendor. **Qualifications and Personal Skills:** * Relevant experience in IT Support or Service. * Drivers licence. * Office 365 Administration. * Knowledge of Active Directory and Server Administration. * Excellent organizational and interpersonal skills. * Excellent customer service skills. * Self\-motivated with an ability to work in teams. * Be enthusiastic, ambitious, and keen to learn new skills with a positive attitude. * Able to prioritise and execute tasks in a high\-pressure environment. * Industry standard certifications (Microsoft, Cisco \& CompTIA). **Some of our Benefits:** * Generous professional development budget. * Company Pension. * Health Plan. * Wellness Benefit. * Onsite Gym. At Nostra we value our people. We are a passionate team committed to doing our best, our values are trust, accountability, expertise, people, integrity, and empathy. These values form a central part of our recruitment process. Nostra is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion.

title: Information Security Officer
description: ***Le Meas Executive Search*** are delighted to partner with an Financial Services firm to hire an Information Technology / Security Officer for their Dublin operation. This newly created position will report to the Head of Information Security to improve our clients Group TOM and to help develop a number of operational IT and Information Security processes that need to be developed and maintained. **Key Areas of Responsibility:** **Information Technology** * Operational, support and end user communication activities related to our clients owned applications and related to shared services provided by Group's Technology Service Provider. * IT related change and adoption activities. * IT Project Portfolio Manager support. * IT Supply \& Contract Manager support. * IT Economics Manager support. * Workplace strategy execution support \& Project Management support. * IT Incident Management support. * IT Change and Adoption Manager support: Support with Change and Adoption Management of new IT Service Provider Services in collaboration with the IT Service Provider Workplace Manager. * Support with the Management of third\-party business applications through IT Service Management processes, including supporting the Business Owners of those business applications with the needed tasks. * Third party control assurance (fit gap assessment). **Information Security** * Information Security related operational and communication activities. * Identity and Access Management Business Operations support, e.g., support with: Administrative execution of business role lifecycle management within Business Operations tooling. * Maintenance of segregation of duties matrices. * Support Line Managers to grant secure user accesses in a timely and efficient manner by implementing and managing a user access model according to the IAM Target Operating Model. * Support operational Information Security processes, e.g., in Vulnerability Management, Cloud Security Posture Management, Third Party Cyber Risk Management. **IT Service Continuity Management (ITSCM)** * Update and maintain the ITSCM framework (fit gap assessment, ITSCM multi \- year test plan, DR reporting, ITSCM strategy). **Cookie Management** * Support the cookie compliance on our clients websites and portals with cookie classification and maintenance activities. * Support with cookie consent management and cookie classification related to our clients portals and websites. **Qualifications:** * L7 degree in computer sciences, business or comparable on the job experience. * ITIL Foundation advantageous. * CompTIA CySA\+ or comparable advantageous. * Minimum of 3 years’ experience in IT / Information Security Operations. * Sound knowledge of ITSM processes. * Sound Information Technology knowledge. * Sound Information Security knowledge. * Pro\-active with ability to work on own initiative and as part of a team. * Self\-manage multiple priorities, routine work and urgent issues. * Good analytical skills, technical knowledge. * Fluency in written and spoken English. * Ability to translate complex technical topics into common language Influential skills that can project genuine excitement about new technologies to a wider audience. Monitoring and testing of delegated activities to partners such as but not limited to **:** Sales and Distribution Processes, Subscriptions Process, Claims and Complaints Handling, Adherence to title: Information Technology Support Engineer
description: My Dublin City Centre based client is recruiting for an IT Support Engineer (Level 2\) to join a growing team. This is a permanent role and will be fully onsite. The IT Support Engineer will support and maintain the firm’s Microsoft\-based IT environment. The role involves providing advanced technical support across Microsoft 365, Azure, desktop systems, networking, and end\-user services in a fast\-paced professional environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide Level 2 support for desktops, laptops, mobile devices, and remote users. * Support Microsoft 365 services including Outlook, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Exchange Online. * Administer Azure / Entra ID, Intune, MFA, and user access management. * Troubleshoot Windows 10/11, networking, VPN, printer, and hardware issues. * Assist with server support, Active Directory, patching, and backups. * Support cybersecurity, endpoint protection, and compliance standards. * Participate in IT projects, upgrades, and continuous improvement initiatives. **Required Skills** * Experience supporting Microsoft 365 and Azure environments. * Strong knowledge of Windows desktop support and troubleshooting. * Experience with Intune / Endpoint Manager and Entra ID. * Understanding of networking fundamentals (DNS, DHCP, VPN, TCP/IP). * Excellent communication and customer service skills. * Experience in a legal or professional services environment desirable. **Qualifications** * IT qualification or equivalent experience. * Microsoft certifications advantageous.

title: Installation Engineer
description: **Who We Are** Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting\-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world. **What We Offer** Location: Dublin,IRL You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more. At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits. As a Field Service Engineer \[Customer Engineer] at Applied Materials, you serve as the direct liaison with customers, collaborating closely to install, maintain, and upgrade equipment. You'll use digital analytics for troubleshooting and apply basic diagnostic techniques to assess and address technical issues. Your responsibilities include performing preventative and corrective maintenance on various systems such as electrical, vacuum, mechanical, plasma, hydraulic, and gas systems. Additionally, you'll coordinate and communicate directly with customers to ensure smooth operations and exceptional service. In order to support our long\-term development, we are looking for talents to join our teams in Europe (Dresden, Germany / Dublin, Ireland / Grenoble, France). Within our Installation department, we are looking for our **Installation Engineer to support European regions** . The iTeam (Installation team) stands for the Installation of semiconductors production equipment across the European Region (mainly in Germany, ireland, France and Italy). We roll in, assemble, connect, and test the new systems until we hand over to our service team and our customers for the manufacturing of wafers to kick off. Being part of the Install Task Force is more than a job, it is a way of life. As a team **we travel approx. 75 % of our working time** , and also get opportunities to support installations worldwide. This position demands technical expertise, customer focus, team involvement and flexibility to travel. **Full training on our equipment will be provided through mentoring.** **Key Responsibilities** * Perform independently installation tasks (mechanical assembly, electrical connections, robot calibrations), from the integration to the inspection of the tools including troubleshooting in case of issues. * Follow all checklists and procedures and ensure correct procedures and schematics are available prior to performing work. * Test equipment with assistance from senior engineers, existing documentation and diagnostic techniques. * Respond to factory issues by communicating with other engineers and participating in escalation conference calls. * Participate in continuous improvement projects to reduce installation cycle time and costs. * Provide accurate reporting of status, hours worked and non\-conformances. * Adhere to the security policy and quality standards of Applied Materials and its customers. **Skills and Experience required:** * Bachelor or Master degree with technical background (mechanical assembly, equipment installation, maintenance, mechatronics, electronics, electrical, mechanical, or similar...) * Position open to junior profiles. * Troubleshooting skills to solve mechanical and electrical issues during system installation. * Ability to communicate daily pass\-downs and equipment status reports. * Project management skills to control installation project within budget, timeline and installation quality requirements. * Ability to work autonomously. * Good English communication skills both written and verbal. * Good Knowledge of the MS Office (Excel, Powerpoint) * The position requires extensive travelling throughout Europe (75% of Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: KYC AML Analyst (12-Month FTC)
description: Who we are: TrueLayer is Europe’s fastest\-growing Pay by Bank network. We power smarter, safer and faster payments by combining real\-time bank payments with financial and identity data. E\-commerce, iGaming and Financial Services businesses use our products to onboard new users, accept money and make payouts in seconds, and at scale. Live across 22 countries and with 1 new user joining TrueLayer’s Pay by Bank network every 3 seconds, we’re in a very exciting growth stage. This is why we’re expanding the team across our offices in London, Milan, and Dublin. Listed in Sifted as one of the 100 fastest growing start\-ups in UK \& Ireland, and CNBC’s World Top Fintech Companies, we’re trusted by industry leaders like RyanAir and Just Eat Takeaway for continued innovation within our payment products. To date, we’ve raised over $300 million from world\-renowned investors including Stripe, Tiger Global, Northzone, and Tencent. But this is just the beginning. We’re looking for people who are excited about joining the Pay by Bank revolution and helping us change the way the world pays. Could this be you? **Description** TrueLayer Underwriting Team is an integral part of our Commercial function, focusing on enabling seamless client experiences during the onboarding journey. This team specialises in Due Diligence/KYC onboarding reviews and AML investigations, contributing to a smooth and compliant onboarding process for our clients. This is a meaningful opportunity for a Due Diligence/KYC Specialist to join TrueLayer in enhancing the financial crime element of the client onboarding process. As a KYC/AML Analyst, you will maintain our first line of defence against money laundering and financial terrorism while supporting the rapid growth of our business. Your work will protect TrueLayer from financial crime exposure, ensuring that we enter and maintain business relationships that align with our compliance obligations and ethical standards. Sitting within a client\-facing team under the Commercial function, this role provides valuable insight into our customer base and the products available to them. We are looking for a Due Diligence/KYC subject matter expert who is well\-versed in financial crime, with a clear focus on KYC and due diligence processes. **What we expect from you as a KYC/AML Analyst:** * Conduct KYB and KYC checks on prospective customers. * Complete Due Diligence on high\-risk and complex customer use cases at onboarding to assess and manage the financial crime risks of new business relationships. * Identify and communicate financial crime risks for prospective customers, ensuring that the customer risk profile falls within TrueLayer’s risk appetite. * Perform Customer Screening, including PEP, Sanction and adverse media checks. * Perform on\-going monitoring on customers and associated individuals \- working PEP and sanctions alerts and ensuring rationale and outcomes are recorded. * Conduct periodic reviews and event triggered reviews where required. * Complete peer reviews on onboarding and ongoing DD review cases. * Conduct agent onboarding and monitoring of agents on an ongoing basis. * Act as a subject matter expert and escalation point for financial crime issues. * Contribute to the ongoing improvement of systems and processes. * Be comfortable working to/ setting KPIs and support in the accurate collation of MI for Senior Management Team/ external reporting. **Requirements** **About you** * Experience conducting due diligence (DD) reviews on corporate customers including conducting KYC checks, customer screening (PEP and sanctions checks) and assessing financial crime risks. * Experience dealing with complex business models and a range of different business customer types. * Good working knowledge of Financial Crime risks, AML controls and KYC/CDD best practices. * Ability to act as an SME for 1LOD DD, supporting other business areas with financial crime matters. * Comfortable working with other functions and questioning or escalating, where appropriate. * Attention to detail, with an interest in learning and engaging with others. * Experience in optimising due diligence processes and procedures. **We would be particularly interested if you have:** * Having experience onboarding eCommerce merchants is valued, as well as with iGaming/gambling or cryptocurrency businesses. * Experience in Finance, a Financial Technology company or a technology company; * Experience in a progressive and innovative technology startup. * Experience in working with various KYC and authentication software providers. **Don’t meet all the requirements?** Multiple studies have shown that women and people of underrepresented groups are sometimes less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single requirement. At Truelayer, we are committed to building a diverse workforce, so if you are excited about this role and have the essential skills, but not the entire checklist \- we’d still love to hear from you! **Benefits** **What you can expect from us:** * Flexible hours and hybrid working. Split your time 50/50 between working from home and our welcoming offices in Dublin 🇮🇪 spending 2\-3 days per week in the office. * Need to collect the kids from childcare? Love a workout in the gym first thing? No worries, we trust you to do your best work within our hybrid framework 🧘 * Prorated \- 24 days holiday as standard ✈️ with flexible bank holidays, so you can take those days whenever you like 🌍 * Prorated \- 12 fully\-paid wellbeing days a year and your birthday off (on top of the holiday allowance) 🕊️ * Life Cover as part of our benefits package, offering financial protection and peace of mind for you and your loved ones 🛟 * Prorated \- Generous parental leave, above and beyond statutory requirements 👪 * Competitive pension contribution at 4% \& 4% 🧓 * Private health insurance from the day you start 🧑‍⚕️ * Membership of mental wellbeing platform Spill and premium Calm subscription * Prorated \- A €1000 budget to spend on learning \& development each year 📚 * Free lunch from Just Eat 🥙 up to 3 days a week when you are in the office At TrueLayer, we don’t just do inclusion and diversity. We embrace people that have different opinions, perspectives and personalities. Because we believe that by seeing the world from all sorts of angles, we can make life better for all the people who live in it. We strongly encourage applications from underrepresented groups (e.g. people of colour, the LGBTQ\+ community, people with disabilities, neurodivergent people, parents, carers, and people from all socio\-economic backgrounds). If you’d like to discuss alternative working patterns, please let us know. We will always aim to make appropriate adjustments to ensure we are fully inclusive to people with different needs during our interview process. So if you need us to make any adjustments to suit your individual needs please let us know \- we’ll be happy to support you.

title: LDA Project Quantity Surveyor
description: **Your new company** We are currently assisting the Land Development Agency with their recruitment processes. The Land Development Agency (LDA) is the State's affordable housing delivery body. It delivers affordable homes on State\-owned or acquired land and through homebuilder partnerships as part of its Project Tosaigh initiative. The Land Development Agency's purpose is to maximise the supply of affordable homes on State and other land in a financially sustainable manner, supporting the creation of thriving communities and delivering ongoing positive social impact. Current Pipeline Snapshot: * 21,000\+ homes being assessed \& advanced across 40\+ sites (direct delivery) * 5,000\+ homes under construction (direct delivery) * 8,000\+ homes targeted delivery through the LDA Project Tosaigh initiative by 2028 * 3,000\+ people living in LDA provide affordable homes at present. The above represents the current pace of delivery to the LDA's target of delivering c. 4,000 homes per year over the coming years. These projects and homes comprise developments relating to low\-mid density housing, high\-density mixed\-use schemes and adaptive reuse of existing and heritage/protected buildings in city centre and sub\-urban locations throughout all of Ireland. **Your new role** Do you want to further develop your professional career across the full spectrum of residential property development? Do you want to join a highly motivated team of professionals working towards a common goal? Do you want to apply your core quantity surveying skills in an innovative and exciting way? This is an exciting opportunity for a dynamic quantity surveyor/cost manager with strong commercial and technical acumen to join the Cost Management Team and to support the development of the LDA's extensive development pipeline, one of the most significant development pipelines in the history of the state. The role will involve providing in\-house cost management expertise and support across multiple business functions including Strategic Planning \& Master Planning, Land Acquisition, Property Development, Construction Delivery, Developer Collaboration, Asset Management and Investment. **What to Expect** Innovation * Work in a forward\-looking environment using market leading platforms and systems to streamline QS and property development workflows. * Enjoy a collaborative and innovative culture that values fresh thinking and initiative while having opportunity to affect policy change. Sector Expert * Gain deep insight and hands\-on experience of fundamental design \& cost economics for residential development developing industry leading expertise. * Involvement in all stages of the development lifecycle \- from concept to delivery to operational management of the schemes. * Enhance experience of varying procurement models, contract and claims management across LDA portfolio involving D\&B, Employer Designed, Early Contractor Involvement, Bespoke Development Partnership agreements, Consultancy Agreements etc. * Gain key skills in detailed cost and investment modelling and viability analyses at each stage of a development's lifecycle. Network * Work alongside senior leaders of industry from all disciplines including design consultants, main contractors, developers, senior public representatives, planning, legal and sales agents offering a unique holistic view of how major developments evolve and become a reality. * Contribute and influence strategic planning and commercial decision\-making at senior level. * Regular contributions to industry and national bodies. Growth * This is not a standard QS role \- Join a rapidly growing organisation and enjoy a varied and evolving remit. You'll have the freedom to explore areas that interest you, whether that's design innovation, procurement and contract strategy, technology, modern methods of construction, site acquisition appraisals, investment modelling and sales and asset management. * Exposure to the full range of property disciplines employed by the agency with open door policy for on\-the\-job insights. * Regular training and CDP with dedicated in\-house HR Learning \& Development team. * 3rd level training subsistence available and encouraged to enhance and turbo charge career. * Guided mentorship with dedicated SCSI APC supervisor(s) and counsellor(s) to get you to the next level. **Key Responsibilities** * Visit sites in consideration and preparation of Order of Magnitude Cost Estimates and Development Budgets for early\-stage viability analysis. * Viability and cost modelling analyses. * Procurement of project design teams and construction delivery partners. * Interface and liaise closely with external consultants, building effective and collaborative working relationships. * Contributing to design team workshops offering cost, value management, procurement, contract and affordability advice. * Management of project development budget and post contract expenditure. * Liaise closely with all LDA external quantity surveying consultants. Review and assess external QS reports and cost documents. * Assisting LDA Development Managers and Construction Managers as required in the management of projects on a day\-to\-day basis. * Involvement in supply chain (Main Contractor/Developer/Consultant) negotiations where appropriate. * Maintaining and enhancing current cost databases of construction and development costs. * Ensuring development budgets and specific project gateway budget approval requests are robust. * Assist and contribute to enhancement and optimisation of LDA cost management standardised documentation, processes and function. * Assist in financial claims analyses and appraisal of construction and development soft costs. **What you'll need to succeed** * Indicative level of 2 to 8 year's demonstrable relevant experience with a Contractor, PQS or Developer. * Previous experience of residential developments preferred but not essential. * A dynamic self\-starter, ability to take ownership for assigned responsibilities and deliverables. * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Strong interpersonal, presentation and verbal communication skills. * Be a team player with ability to work independently and collaboratively across both internal and external teams. * Microsoft Office Suite, with particular emphasis on strong MS Excel abilities. * Degree or higher in Quantity Surveying. * Chartered or willingness/in the process of gaining chartership. **What you'll get in return** In return, you will join the LDA who play an essential role in delivering some of Ireland's most critical infrastructure over the coming decades. The organisation is committed to excellence, with a strong focus on continuous learning, professional development, and long\-term career progression. You will collaborate with and lead a high\-performing team of construction, commercial, and development professionals who are passionate about delivering meaningful impact. The role offers a competitive salary, depending on experience, along with hybrid working arrangements to support work\-life balance. While primarily office\-based, the role includes site visits as required. Standard working hours are under 40 per week, Monday to Friday, with flexibility where appropriate. Additional benefits include a discretionary performance bonus, industry\-leading employer pension contributions, and significant opportunities for advancement within a purpose\-driven and rapidly growing organisation. This is more than just a job \- it is a long\-term career opportunity with real purpose and national significance. **What you need to do now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career. Hays Ireland is a trading division of Hays Specialist Recruitment (Ireland) Limited and acts as an employment agency for permanent recruitment and employment business for the supply of temporary workers. By applying for this job you accept the T\&C's, Privacy Policy and Disclaimers which can be found at hays.co.uk

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: Legal Secretary
description: Legal Secretaries with experience in litigation and seeking a hybrid working role \- this one is for you! We, here at Search Talent, are currently hiring a legal secretary to work in the commercial litigation department for a reputable and much sought after top tier firm in Dublin City Centre. We are seeking someone with 1\+ years experience as a legal secretary with experience a broad form of litigation (be it commercial, personal injury or insurance etc), who is now open to taking their career to the next level with a top level firm. This role is 3 days in office, 2 from home. Salary range dependent on experience but potential for 45\-50k. **A little bit about the firm:** * A full service law firm. * A head count of 100\+ * A Firm Currently attracting progressively more and more much sought after clientele and are continuously growing their pool of clients at the moment, with significant names in both the private and public sector. **A Little bit about you:** * You are not doing FE1s nor have ambitions to become a solicitor in the foreseeable future. * 1\+ years secretarial experience in a commercial litigation department * Strong communication skills and good telephone manner * Experience managing a portfolio of senior stakeholders within the legal field. * MS Office skills are essential. * Meticulous attention to detail. * Be able to work on own initiative and as part of a team **Duties \& Responsibilities** * Provide comprehensive administrative support including document drafting, diary management, and general legal administration (scanning, printing, photocopying). * Manage the end\-to\-end file lifecycle using the firm’s legal case management system. * Prepare materials and information for internal and external meetings. * Liaise with internal and external stakeholders to understand and respond to their requirements. * Maintain discretion and confidentiality when handling sensitive information. * Ensure accuracy and speed in document production, coordinating with document specialists when required. * Manage travel arrangements and detailed itineraries. * Process time records and expense reports. * Greet and liaise with clients and business visitors in a professional manner We at Search Talent are encouraging anyone who even wants to consider a fantastic and hybrid option for them in the legal secretarial market, and with a background as a legal secretary in this area, to come into contact and we can discuss if this is the best option for you before taking a step further. Reach out to David O’Beirne, Principal Legal Recruitment Consultant, on 019639312 or at davidob@searchtalent.ie for further details.

title: Linux Kernel Engineer
description: **Summary** At Apple, we craft experiences that have transformed entire industries. The diversity of our people and ideas fuels innovation in everything we do. Imagine what you could build here. Join our Linux Kernel Engineering team and play a central role in the reliability, performance, and evolution of Apple’s platforms. As a Linux Kernel Engineer, you will own the kernel lifecycle end to end, from CI infrastructure and automated validation to driver enablement, debugging, and production\-quality releases. You will partner closely with platform, infrastructure, and SRE teams to ensure every kernel we ship is stable, observable, and ready for real world workloads at scale. **Description** In this role, you will design, build, and operate CI and validation pipelines that compile, test, and release Linux kernels across multiple hardware platforms and Linux distributions. You will lead triage of kernel regressions, investigate crashes and performance issues, drive fixes to resolution, and qualify new hardware at the kernel layer. You will collaborate with engineers across Apple to port, maintain, and upstream device drivers, shepherd kernel changes into production, and continuously raise the bar on kernel quality, automation, and release discipline. Your work will have direct impact on the stability, performance, and debuggability of systems that power critical Apple services. If you enjoy deep kernel debugging, building robust CI systems, and owning kernel releases from development through production, we would like to hear from you. **Minimum Qualifications** * Experience developing or maintaining the Linux kernel or low\-level system software (e.g., kernel modules, device drivers, or core OS components). * Proven ability to debug kernel level issues (panics, hangs, performance regressions) using tools such as perf, ftrace, bpftrace, crash, gdb, or similar. * Deep understanding of kernel tuning for stability and performance across many subsystem domains. * Hands\-on experience developing CI/CD systems integrating operating system components and managing kernel releases. * Strong programming skills in C, plus proficiency in at least one of Rust, Go, Python, or Bash. * Solid understanding of operating system fundamentals: processes, memory management, filesystems, networking, scheduling, interrupts, and hardware/driver interactions. * Experience working in a RHEL based Linux environment, including RPM packaging and the RHEL kernel back port and release process. * Strong collaboration and communication skills; comfortable working with cross\-functional teams (SRE, platform, hardware, security). **Preferred Qualifications** * Proven track record of upstream contributions to the Linux kernel project or similar upstreams. * Bachelor of Computer Science or equivalent skills and work experience. At Apple, we believe accessibility is a fundamental human right. You’ll find that idea reflected in everything here — in our culture, our benefits and our digital tools. By welcoming as many perspectives as possible, we help you build a career where you feel like you belong. Learn about accessibility in Apple’s workplace Role Number: 200651433\-0562

That means you already have the core skills of a Recruitment Consultant and now it’s time to use them in a career with real growth, real autonomy, and real earning potential. Our progression path is transparent, fast, and built for people who want to make an impact. * **A Team of High Achievers** \- You’ll join a group of ambitious, driven professionals who came from retail just like you. This is an environment that pushes you, supports you, and celebrates your wins. **Why Retail Talent Thrives Here** * **Fixed Schedule** \- Say goodbye to weekend shifts and unpredictable hours. Enjoy a structured Monday–Friday workweek and reclaim your personal life. * **Individual Targets** \- Your success is in your hands. No more carrying a team \- you own your performance and your results. * **Leverage Your Client Expertise** \- Your ability to connect, influence, and deliver exceptional service becomes your biggest advantage in recruitment. **Compensation** * **Total Earnings Year 1: €45K\+, Year 2: €75K\+, Year 3: €130K** * Base Salary (Early Thirties \- DOE) \+ Commission \+ Bonus **What We Look For** * 2\+ years’ experience in luxury retail: Sales Associate, Sales Consultant, Client Advisor, Merchandising Manager, Assistant Manager, Sales Representative, or similar * A strong desire to transition into a stable, high‑growth corporate career * Based in Dublin * EU citizenship or Stamp 4 visa **Location:** Dublin (on\-site, full\-time) **Eligibility:** Open to EU citizens and Stamp 4 visa holders only. title: Major Incidents/Problem Management
description: **JOB DESCRIPTION** You have the opportunity to drive meaningful change in our digital banking operations. We empower you to shape a culture of continuous improvement and operational excellence. Your skills will help us reduce repeat incidents, enhance customer experience, and support our regulatory commitments. Join a collaborative team where learning and growth are valued. Your voice matters and your impact is celebrated. **Job Summary:** As a Problem \& Incident Manager in the Chase International Consumer Bank team, you will evolve the Problem Management lifecycle for our UK digital bank while carrying substantial Incident Management responsibility. You lead post\-incident reviews, drive rigorous root cause analysis, and define corrective actions to reduce repeat incidents and strengthen operational resilience. You combine reactive and proactive problem management, using data and trend analysis to address issues before they impact customers. You own and execute the Incident Management process for major incidents, ensuring swift restoration and clear communication. You partner with cross\-functional teams to implement long\-term solutions and foster a culture of learning and improvement. **Job Responsibilities:** * Lead post\-incident reviews from preparation to high\-quality root cause analysis documentation * Apply industry\-recognized RCA techniques to ensure evidence\-based conclusions * Develop, track, and validate corrective action plans, escalating blockers as needed * Govern Problem Management processes and coach teams on best practices * Execute reactive problem management by triaging and prioritizing problem records * Execute proactive problem management by analyzing data to identify trends and resilience gaps * Partner with teams to embed long\-term solutions and coordinate change management * Facilitate learning reviews and promote shared accountability for continuous improvement * Report on Problem Management metrics and trends to senior stakeholders * Build strong relationships with engineering, SRE, operations, risk, compliance, and vendors * Respond to escalations, own the Incident Management process, and maintain authoritative timelines **Required Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills:** * Experience leading Problem Management in high\-availability, high\-transaction environments * Expertise in RCA methodologies and evidence\-based analysis * Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret observability signals and technical findings * Deep familiarity with ITIL practices, especially Problem, Incident, and Change * Excellent real\-time communication under pressure, including regulator\-facing updates * Experience facilitating major incidents and leading cross\-functional incident bridges * Ability to triage and manage multiple simultaneous incidents, prioritizing by impact * Strong situational leadership, decision\-making, time management under pressure and ability to influence and drive cross\-team actions without direct authority * Customer\-centric mindset with sound judgment on business and regulatory impacts * Familiarity with monitoring, alerting, and observability toolchains * Proficiency with collaboration and workflow tools such as ServiceNow, Jira, Confluence, and customer communication tooling **Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills:** * ITIL certification and/or qualifications in Lean Six Sigma, reliability engineering, or similar disciplines * Experience working with agile teams, including backlog management and outcome\-based delivery * Familiarity with cloud\-native architectures, microservices, APIs, and modern SRE practices * Experience engaging external vendors in RCAs and corrective action delivery * Knowledge of operational resilience frameworks and risk management in regulated environments * Prior experience in Major Incident Management or Command Centre operations * Experience coordinating customer communications during incidents **ABOUT US** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **ABOUT THE TEAM** Our professionals in our Corporate Functions cover a diverse range of areas from finance and risk to human resources and marketing. Our corporate teams are an essential part of our company, ensuring that we’re setting our businesses, clients, customers and employees up for success.

title: Manufacturing Operator
description: McDermott Laboratories Limited Viatris is a global healthcare company uniquely positioned to bridge the traditional divide between generics and brands, combining the best of both to more holistically address healthcare needs globally. With a mission to empower people worldwide to live healthier at every stage of life, we provide access at scale, currently supplying high\-quality medicines to approximately 1 billion patients around the world annually and touching all of life's moments, from birth to the end of life, acute conditions to chronic diseases. We have been included on number of award lists that demonstrate the impact we are making. Our global portfolio includes best\-in\-class, iconic brand\-name products as well as global key brands; generics, including branded and complex generics; and other offerings across a wide range of therapeutic areas. We are committed to helping create healthier communities worldwide through education, outreach and better access to treatment. For those driven to live their lives with purpose, Viatris helps employees realise their full potential by creating a collaborative culture that fosters courage and resilience. Viatris is more than just a place to work. This is a place to make a difference in the world. Every day, we rise to the challenge to make a difference and here’s how the Manufacturing Operator role will make an impact: Key responsibilities for this role include: * Operate manufacturing lines in efficient manner in accordance with the relevant SOP’s to ensure required output targets are achieve * Ensure that the manufacturing equipment and area is cleaned to GMP standards prior to use * Responsible for the preparatory set up machines and equipment, and product in process testing, and for product condition during processing. * Complete the Batch Manufacturing Record and the reconciliations of the BMR according to relevant S.O.P.\`s. * Report directly to Manufacturing Supervisor or Manager, if any problems or damage occurs. * Responsible for a high standard of GMP and good housekeeping, and for inspection of batches that do not meet the quality standards. * Responsible for the handling and storage of Controlled Drugs in accordance with relevant procedures, ensuring product security. * Adhere to all company and legislative health, safety and environmental requirements, and perform any other duties as assigned by Manufacturing Supervisor / Manager. * User of Manufacturing support software packages e.g. MES, SAP, Trackwise, Documentum etc. * Constantly look to improve the process and to learn new skills The minimum qualifications for this role are: * Leaving Certificate or equivalent * Candidates are required to be able to work different shift patterns plus overtime when required. (4 Cycle, 24\-7 shift pattern) * Candidates are required to have a good understanding of the requirements of Good Manufacturing Practices. (Ideally candidates have experience of working in a GMP environment). * Demonstrated strong team\-working skills * Continuous improvement mindset. * Willing to learn and to continue learning. * Positive, can\-do attitude * Excellent time keeping skills At Viatris, we are dedicated to building a truly diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t fully align with every requirement, we still encourage you to apply. You may just be the right candidate for this or other roles. **Diversity \& Inclusion at Viatris** At Viatris, diversity and inclusion are essential to our mission. The diversity we foster in all aspects of our business can be one of our greatest strengths in redefining healthcare not as it is, but as it should be. If you would like to know more about what diversity, equity and inclusion means to us, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/Careers/Diversity\-and\-Inclusion **Sustainability at Viatris** Corporate social responsibility is fundamental to the Viatris mission. We work to advance responsible and sustainable operations and leverage our collective expertise to empower people to live healthier at every stage of life, recognizing that our actions affect the stakeholders and communities we serve. To learn more about our efforts, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/about\-us/corporate\-responsibility At Viatris, we offer competitive salaries, benefits and an inclusive environment where you can use your experiences, perspectives and skills to help make an impact on the lives of others. Viatris is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Mechanical Design Engineer
description: Mechanical Design Engineer, Blanchardstown, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.
