We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Responsible for ensuring technical compliance on all assigned projects. * Responsible for ensuring scope of works are completed for mechanical packages * Responsible for managing consultant selections and agreeing RACI matrices * Attending design team meetings, in person or through MSTF teams * Responsible for ensuring design programme are aligned with planning teams. * Complete reviews of client responsibility matrices and ensure alignment with project/commercial team. * Responsible for management of RIBA3\-5 design processes and procedures. * Responsible for producing design management plan for projects * Responsible for ensuring Central engineering checklists are implemented on each project * Management of design for safe manufacturing assembly if implemented when required on the projects * Responsible for ensuring below trackers are managed correctly from start\-handover of project. * Procurement schedule * Technical submittal register (CSA) * Drawing register (CSA) * RFI log * Deviation tracker * Lessons learned log * Design Change Log * Monitor all schedule and trackers throughout the course of assigned project * Carry out technical submittal \& working drawing reviews with the teams on a scheduled basis * Assist with workload on the projects you are involved with (Technical submittals, Drawing reviews, RFI’s etc) * Assist the project teams with better buying – Review orders and ensure best price is obtained. * Challenge the consultants and Mercury teams to value engineer. * Responsible for enring compliance with ISO19650 requirements. * Produce report for the projects you are assigned to and issue to direct manager at agreed periods. * Assist with tenders for new projects when required. * Assisting the estimating dept and pe\-construction team at agreed periods * Assist with setting up the value engineering log for the DC\&BS group. * All other tasks related to Design role that is requested by your direct line manager. * Attend Central Engineering stage 5 weekly model review meeting. * Responsible for ensuring the project undergoes a lessons learned review at design stage. * Coaching graduate engineers on projects, how to produce tech subs, manage drawing production, issuing of RFI’s, dealing with clients. * Attend projects within Europe for site visits from time to time. * Attend monthly Central Engineering F2F meeting. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Third level qualification in Mechanical Engineering/ Mechanical Design Engineering * 3\+ years experience working on large industrial plant projects * Proficiency in a related Mechanical Design package/ software * Excellent communication skills * Pride in the quality and presentation of work * Must be able to work from our Dublin Headquarters **Desirable Criteria** * Experience working on datacentres would be an advantage but not essential Mercury is an equal opportunities employer. Apply Now

title: Microsoft Cloud Engineer
description: **Microsoft Cloud Engineer** Dublin (Hybrid working with 2–3 days in the office per week) **The Organisation** Our client is a leading managed IT service provider, delivering reliable and proactive IT support, infrastructure and cloud solutions to businesses across Ireland. They are now looking for a Microsoft Cloud Engineer to join them on a full\-time, permanent basis. **The Benefits** \- Competitive salary \- Structured Microsoft certification pathway with all exam fees covered \- Direct mentorship from the Head of Cloud \- Clear progression framework from L2 Engineer to L3 Engineer and Cloud Architect \- Opportunity to build and own AVD environments from the ground up \- 25 days’ annual leave \- Pension contribution \- Collaborative, growing team environment \- On\-call supplement provided This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced MSP engineer to deepen their expertise across Azure Infrastructure, Microsoft 365 and Azure Virtual Desktop with one of Ireland’s most accredited Microsoft partners. You’ll have the chance to gain significant exposure to complex cloud environments, project delivery and high\-level technical decision\-making, enhancing your portfolio and building your skills. What’s more, with structured certification support, project ownership and a clearly defined progression pathway, this role is perfect for the technically brilliant, the ambitious, and the target\-driven alike. **The Role** As a Microsoft Cloud Engineer, you'll be tasked with delivering level 2 support to a range of customers, focusing on cloud services. This role combines reactive support escalations with proactive project delivery. Approximately 70% of time will be spent on support escalations across Azure infrastructure, Microsoft 365, and third\-party software, with the remaining 30% dedicated to project work, including new AVD deployments, environment upgrades, and lifecycle management. title: Mid Market Account Executive
description: **SurveyMonkey is the world’s most popular platform for surveys and forms, built for business—loved by users.** We combine powerful capabilities with intuitive design, effectively serving every use case, from customer experience to employee engagement, market research to payment and registration forms. With built\-in research expertise and AI\-powered technology, it’s like having a team of expert researchers at your fingertips. Trusted by millions—from startups to Fortune 500 companies—SurveyMonkey helps teams gather insights and information that inspire better decisions, create experiences people love, and drive business growth. You will identify unique mission\-driven needs and build ROI\-based business cases for diverse public and private sector stakeholders while utilizing AI and different Sales platforms to maintain a disciplined pipeline from initial prospecting through to close. **What You’ll Be Working On** * Full\-Cycle Sales Ownership: Manage the entire sales motion from initial prospecting and outbound outreach to consultative positioning, complex negotiation, and closing. * Strategic Pipeline Development: Identify and qualify high\-value prospects and expansion opportunities using tools like ZoomInfo and Outreach. * Methodology\-Driven Selling: Apply the Sandler selling methodology and MEDDPICC framework to uncover customer needs, map capabilities, and articulate ROI. * Data\-Informed Deal Management: Utilize Salesforce and Gong to track deal progress, maintain accurate forecasting, and refine strategies based on interaction insights. * Territory Strategy: Prioritize accounts and manage multiple workstreams concurrently to consistently achieve quota and maintain a healthy pipeline. **We’d love to hear from people with** * B2B Sales Experience: 2–5\+ years of full\-cycle sales experience, specifically navigating complex organizational structures (experience selling to Marketing, HR, or IT is a plus). * Proven Multi\-Channel Prospecting: A track record of generating pipeline through outbound value\-selling, inbound marketing leads, and product\-led growth signals. * Methodology Proficiency: Demonstrated success applying MEDDPICC or a comparable framework to manage stakeholders and close enterprise\-level deals. * Sales Tech Stack Expertise: Direct experience using Salesforce, Outreach, Gong, and ZoomInfo to manage a book of business. * AI\-First Mindset: Proactive in adopting AI productivity tools to automate administrative tasks, allowing for more "high\-value" human interaction and deeper strategic account planning. * Value\-Based Selling Skills: Experience building formal business cases, developing internal Champions, and executing Mutual Action Plans (MAPs). SurveyMonkey believes in\-person collaboration is valuable for building relationships, fostering community, and enhancing our speed and execution in problem\-solving and decision\-making. **As such, you will be required to work from our Dublin office up to 1 day per week.** **Why SurveyMonkey? We’re a global company where people from all backgrounds can make an impact, build meaningful connections, and grow their careers. Our teams work in a flexible, hybrid environment with thoughtfully designed offices and programs like the CHOICE Fund to help employees thrive in work and life. Our milestones include celebrating a quarter\-century of curiosity with 25 acts of giving, opening new hubs in Costa Rica and India, crossing the threshold of 100 billion questions answered, and earning recognition as one of the Most Inspiring Workplaces across North America and Asia. We live our company values—like championing inclusion and making it happen—by embedding them into how we hire, collaborate, and grow. They help shape everything from our culture to our business decisions. Come join us and see where your curiosity can take you. **Our commitment to an inclusive workplace** SurveyMonkey is an equal opportunity employer committed to providing a workplace free from harassment and discrimination. We celebrate the unique differences of our employees because that is what drives curiosity, innovation, and the success of our business. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status, pregnancy, parental status, genetic information, political affiliation, or any other status protected by the laws or regulations in the locations where we operate. title: Mid-Market Account Executive (Baltics Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We’re looking for a Mid\-Market Account Executive who’s a people\-focused expert at identifying prospective mid\-market customers. With detailed knowledge of our full product suite, you'll match customers to the best plan based on their needs, helping them to adopt new features and increase usage of existing ones. If you’re a motivated individual who enjoys a challenge and acts with integrity at all times, we’d love to hear from you. **What You'll Be Doing** * Activating new customers in the mid\-market segment * Prospecting mid\-market accounts by identifying high\-potential customers based on key indicators, such as annual turnover, number of employees, nature of the business, etc. * Conducting discovery and qualification to assess customer needs and potential during the sign\-up process, while providing support and guidance * Collaborating with Product Sales Executives to deepen customer relationships by sharing relevant insights to cross\-sell as appropriate, ensuring a robust product feedback loop **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English and Russian * 1\+ years of experience as a Sales or Business Development Representative * 3\+ years of experience as an Account Executive managing the full sales cycle * A track record of closing high\-value deals with medium to long sales cycles * Solid prospecting skills to generate leads and identify and pursue new business opportunities * Experience in fast\-paced tech environments, ideally fintech, with basic knowledge of local competitors * Expert relationship\-building skills to interact with multiple high\-level decision makers of an organisation, ideally C\-suite (CFOs, Heads of Treasury and Finance, etc.) * Excellent communication skills to clearly articulate product capabilities to meet specific needs of large customers * The ability to collaborate cross\-functionally with teams, such as Product and Support **Nice to have** * Fluency in another European language * Experience selling financial products and features * Experience using Salesforce Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. title: Mobile Technician

title: Network Architect (Azure)
description: Senior Network Architect (Azure) Role Overview We are seeking an experienced Network Architect with deep expertise in Microsoft Azure to design, implement, and optimize scalable, secure, and high\-performance network solutions. This role will be responsible for shaping cloud network strategy, ensuring best practices, and supporting complex hybrid and multi\-cloud environments. Key Responsibilities * Design and architect enterprise\-grade network solutions within Microsoft Azure, including hybrid connectivity. * Lead the development of network architecture standards, policies, and best practices. * Implement and manage Azure networking services such as Virtual Networks (VNets), VPN Gateway, ExpressRoute, Azure Firewall, Load Balancers, and Application Gateway. * Design secure connectivity between on\-premises infrastructure and Azure environments. * Optimize network performance, scalability, and cost\-efficiency across cloud environments. * Collaborate with security teams to enforce Zero Trust architecture and compliance requirements. * Provide technical leadership and mentorship to network engineers and cloud teams. * Troubleshoot complex networking issues across hybrid and cloud\-native environments. * Evaluate emerging technologies and recommend improvements to existing architecture. * Produce and maintain detailed architecture documentation and diagrams. Preferred Qualifications * Microsoft certifications such as: * Azure Solutions Architect Expert * Azure Network Engineer Associate * Experience with multi\-cloud environments (AWS, GCP). * Knowledge of SD\-WAN and network virtualization technologies. * Experience with DevOps practices and CI/CD pipelines. * Scripting skills (PowerShell, Python). title: People Experience Administrator

Our culture is built on curiosity, collaboration and craic where every idea counts and every journey matters. Ready to make an impact? Let’s go places together. CarTrawler’s People team shapes an environment where talent thrives and culture drives performance. From recruitment and learning to engagement and inclusion, we connect people to purpose and potential. We’re passionate about making CarTrawler a place where every team member can grow, contribute and make an impact. **Your Journey starts here!** Help shape a seamless and data\-driven employee experience across CarTrawler. As part of our collaborative People Experience team, this role combines HR operations, systems expertise and people analytics to support employees and managers throughout the employee lifecycle. From onboarding and payroll administration to workforce reporting and HR systems optimisation, you’ll play a key role in ensuring our People processes are accurate, efficient and scalable. You’ll work closely with teams across the business to turn people data into meaningful insights that support smarter decisions, stronger performance and a better employee experience for all. **What You’ll Do As a People Experience Administrator** * Deliver a smooth and consistent employee experience across onboarding, employee changes and offboarding processes * Act as a trusted point of contact for employees and managers, supporting HR systems, policies and people\-related queries * Maintain and optimise employee data within HR systems, ensuring accuracy, compliance and strong data governance * Support the monthly payroll process by coordinating updates, changes and related administration in a timely and accurate manner * Create and maintain HR dashboards, reports and workforce insights using tools such as Excel and Power BI * Analyse trends across engagement, attrition, hiring, absence and performance to identify opportunities for improvement * Support performance and development processes, including review cycles, probation tracking and completion monitoring * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives across HR operations, systems and reporting capabilities * Collaborate closely with the wider People team and business stakeholders to support strategic People initiatives * Explore opportunities to leverage AI and emerging technologies to improve efficiency, reporting and decision\-making across People operations **What You’ll Bring To The Team** * 3\+ years’ experience in HR administration, People Operations, HR analytics or a similar People\-focused role * Strong analytical skills with experience interpreting and presenting workforce data and insights * Advanced Excel skills and experience using reporting or visualisation tools such as Power BI * Hands\-on experience with HR systems, ideally including HiBob and Lever * Excellent attention to detail with a focus on accuracy, confidentiality and continuous improvement * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to support employees and managers across all levels * Experience working in a fast\-paced environment with the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively * A collaborative and proactive mindset with a passion for improving employee experience and operational excellence * Familiarity with engagement surveys, workforce metrics and people analytics methodologies is an advantage * Relevant qualification in HR, Business, Analytics or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience **Go on, give it a go!** We know that looking for a new job can feel a bit daunting but don’t let a touch of imposter syndrome hold you back. Even if you don’t tick every box, we’d still love to hear from you! Apply today to stay connected. At CarTrawler, we’re proud to be an equal opportunities employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for everyone. No matter your background, identity, or life experience, if you’re passionate about what you do, you’ll be welcomed here. **Perks for the journey** Competitive pay and benefits, including a performance\-based annual bonus, fully subsidised VHI health insurance and pension matching up to 4%. Generous time off and flexible working, including 25 days’ annual leave, extra company days, monthly Friday Unplugged afternoons and the option to work abroad for up to 20 days a year. Comprehensive wellbeing support, including access to health and mental health programmes (HeadsUp and EAP) and paid leave for marriage, volunteering and personal wellbeing including menstrual, menopause, and fertility leave. Growth and recognition culture with development opportunities through training, coaching and study support plus programmes that celebrate individual and team achievements. Everyday perks that make a difference \- subsidised canteen (KC Peaches), on\-site car parking, Bike to Work and TaxSaver schemes plus an active Sports \& Social Club. We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Personal Assistant
description: **Personal Assistant \- MNC – Dublin** A well\-established MNC is seeking to appoint an experienced and highly organised **Personal Assistant** to support senior leadership across a broad range of business and personal administration activities. This is a varied and fast\-paced role offering exposure within a professional, discreet, and dynamic environment. The successful candidate will be proactive, detail\-oriented, and comfortable managing multiple priorities while maintaining the highest level of confidentiality. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide day\-to\-day administrative and organisational support to senior stakeholders * Manage complex diaries, meetings, and scheduling requirements * Coordinate domestic and international travel arrangements, itineraries, and accommodation * Handle correspondence, calls, and email management * Prepare documents, presentations, and reports as required * Maintain records, filing systems, and confidential documentation * Liaise with external advisors, suppliers, and service providers * Support office coordination and assist with ad\-hoc projects and personal administration tasks **About You** * Previous experience in a Personal Assistant, Executive Assistant, or similar support role * Highly organised with excellent attention to detail * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * Professional, discreet, and trustworthy * Strong Microsoft Office and general systems skills * Ability to work independently and manage competing priorities in a fast\-paced environment **Remuneration:** Salary DOE, bonus and pension. **If this role isn't for you, but you know someone who would be a great fit, we would appreciate it if you could refer them to us. If we successfully place your friend, you will receive a €250 voucher as a thank you from HireForce.**

title: Platform Engineer (Automation) - Unannounced Project
description: Scopely is looking for a **Senior Platform Engineer(Automation)** to join a new truly unique multiplayer strategy game in Spain, Ireland, Portugal or the UK on a remote or hybrid basis. We can support with visa sponsorship and relocation assistance from any location. At Scopely, we care deeply about what we do and want to inspire play every day \- whether in our work environments alongside our talented colleagues or through our deep connections with our communities of players. We are a global team of game lovers who are developing, publishing and innovating the mobile games industry, connecting millions of people around the world daily. We are in the early stages of development on an ambitious, unannounced Strategy/MMO title, creating a team of talented and passionate game makers to join us on this exciting journey! **What You’ll Do** * We are looking for a software engineer with a strong automation mindset to help improve how our engineering teams builds, tests, and deliver softwares. * This role sits at the intersection of automation, reliability, quality, and developer experience. You’ll work on the systems and tooling that enable teams to ship changes safely and repeatedly, reducing manual effort and operational risk through software. * Design, and evolve automation as internal product, to support the software delivery lifecycle, from code commit through to production * Improve the reliability, consistency, and usability of CI/CD pipelines and supporting tooling * Identify manual, error\-prone, or slow processes workflows and replace them with automation and a product\-driven approach * Collaborate with application engineers, and infrastructure teams to improve delivery safety, resilience, and operational excellence * Contribute to standards and reusable patterns for build, test, deployment, and rollback * Embed reliability, security, and quality checks into automated workflows where they add real value * Improve developer experience through better tooling, documentation, and self\-service capabilities * Use metrics and feedback to guide improvements to delivery performance and system reliability **What We’re Looking For** * Background as a software engineer, SRE, DevOps engineer, or platform engineer with a strong focus on automation * Ability to quickly navigate and understand unfamiliar codebases, proactively identifying and implementing improvements that enhance software engineering patterns (SOLID, DRY), reliability, observability, and overall system health with minimal supervision * Experience improving developer productivity or internal tooling * Experience operating systems across multiple environments (dev, test, prod) * Strong collaboration and communication skills when working across teams * Experience building or maintaining CI/CD pipelines using tools such as GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, or similar * Comfortable writing automation and tooling in a general\-purpose language (e.g. Python, Go, or equivalent) * Working knowledge of cloud environments (AWS, GCP, or Azure), even if not a deep specialist * Familiarity with containerized workloads and modern deployment practices (ECS, Kubernetes/EKS, or similar) **Bonus Points** * Exposure to Site Reliability Engineering practices or operational ownership * Experience working with infrastructure as code tools (Terraform, CloudFormation, or similar) is a plus, not a requirement * Familiarity with delivery performance metrics (e.g. deployment frequency, lead time, failure rates) * Interest in software supply chain security or compliance automation **About Scopely** Scopely is a leading video game and global interactive entertainment company, home to many of the world’s most beloved and enduring experiences, including two of the most successful mobile games of all\-time “MONOPOLY GO!” and “Pokémon GO,” along with “Stumble Guys,” “Star Trek™ Fleet Command,” “MARVEL Strike Force,” “WWE Champions,” the Scrabble® franchise, “Yahtzee® With Buddies,” and many others. Across mobile, web, PC, and console, Scopely creates, develops, publishes, and live\-operates one of the most diversified and award\-winning portfolios in the games industry — bringing hundreds of millions of players together through a shared love of play. Founded in 2011, Scopely is powered by its exceptional team — including thousands of world\-class gamemakers around the globe, a distinctive tenet\-driven culture, and its proprietary technology platform, Playgami. Together, these strengths have fueled Scopely’s position as the \#1 mobile games company in the U.S. and \#2 globally, generating more than $10 billion in lifetime revenue. Whether building global sensations like “MONOPOLY GO!” from the ground up, or expanding through strategic acquisitions, including the FoxNext, GSN, and Niantic games businesses — Scopely consistently delivers experiences players love today and return to for years to come. Recognized multiple times as one of the "100 Most Influential Companies in the World" by TIME magazine and one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies" and “Best Workplaces for Innovators,” Scopely believes that video games can be a force for good — creating meaningful connections, vibrant communities, and making life better through play. Scopely has global operations and partners across four continents in more than a dozen countries worldwide. For more information, visit: https://www.scopely.com/. Notice to Candidates: Scopely will never request payment or financial information during the application or hiring process. Please apply only through our official website and verify that all Talent Partner communications come from an email address ending in @scopely.com. Should you have any questions or encounter any fraudulent requests/emails/websites, please immediately contact recruiting@scopely.com. Our job applicant privacy policies are available here: California Privacy Notice and EEA/UK Privacy Notice. Employment at Scopely is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications. Scopely does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), or any other basis protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

title: Plumber
description: Sensori is expanding and we're looking for a skilled Plumber to join our Mechanical Projects team in Dublin. This is a permanent, full\-time role offering the stability of a growing company, a strong team culture, and the opportunity to work on a varied portfolio of commercial projects. If you take pride in quality workmanship, like working alongside other top trades, and want to be part of a business that's going places \- we want to hear from you. **The Role** As part of Sensori's Mechanical Projects Division, you'll be working on a mix of new\-build and fit\-out projects across the commercial sector in Dublin. You'll be a trusted member of the site team \- delivering quality installations, managing your own work packages, and representing Sensori's standards on every project. Day to day, you can expect to: * Install and commission hot and cold water systems, heating, drainage, and sanitation across a range of commercial buildings * Carry out gas welding, soldering, and brazing to the highest standards of workmanship * Work confidently from technical drawings, design specifications, and project documentation * Review installations to ensure they are fit for purpose and meet design intent and regulatory requirements * Identify material requirements for your work packages and coordinate with the procurement team \- taking ownership of quantities, quality checks, and sign\-off on deliveries * Work closely with the site foreman, main contractors, and other trades, building strong relationships that keep projects running smoothly * Support, guide, and mentor apprentices on site \- contributing to their development and upholding team standards * Proactively identify and resolve on\-site issues before they escalate, communicating clearly with site management * Maintain a safe and organised work environment, adhering to all Health \& Safety legislation and Sensori's quality procedures * Collaborate with the Project Manager and wider team to ensure milestones are hit on time and to the standard our clients expect **What You'll Bring** **Essential:** * QQI Level 6 Advanced Certificate in Plumbing (or equivalent trade qualification) * SOLAS Safe Pass and Manual Handling certificate * Minimum 4 years' experience in a commercial environment * Confident reading technical drawings and construction documentation * A practical, solutions\-focused approach \- able to think on your feet on a busy site * Strong communication skills and a collaborative attitude * Meticulous attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple tasks under pressure **Desirable:** * Experience mentoring or working alongside apprentices * Familiarity with mechanical project environments and multi\-trade coordination

title: Plumbing Inventory Coordinator
description: Our client, a plumbing, heating and mechanical business who work on projects ranging from Educational Institutions, large apartment developments, Retail store, Churches, Hospitals, Office Blocks, Hotels and high spec houses are looking to hire a **Plumbing Inventory Coordinator** for their base in Co. Meath **Responsibilities:** * Manage and follow the **PO (Purchase Order) system** for vendor orders and deliveries. * Order, receive, and track plumbing parts and supplies with accuracy. * Build and maintain **strong vendor relationships** for reliable service and best pricing. * Conduct regular truck stock checks and inventory audits to avoid shortages. * Maintain a clean, safe, and efficient shop environment. * Collaborate with techs and management to ensure everyone has what they need. * Other duties when required **Required:** * Logistics/delivery coordination experience * Deep knowledge of plumbing parts, tools, and materials. * Strong ability to follow and maintain PO systems. * Excellent communication and organization skills. * Positive, professional attitude and great teamwork spirit. * Proven vendor management and relationship\-building skills. * Basic computer or mobile app skills for tracking and ordering. * Valid EU driver’s license

title: Portfolio Associate - Leased, DCC Communities
description: **Description** AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. The AWS Global Data Center Supply Solutions Team is looking for an experienced Portfolio Associate based in the either London or Dublin. In this role, you will be a key member of the Strategic Portfolio Management (SPM) team responsible for tracking and analyzing the operational, financial and contractual performance of our expanding portfolio in Europe, Africa and the Middle East. Portfolio Associates work closely with lessors/vendors and internal partner teams to ensure accuracy of our portfolio data points in order to mitigate risks and support portfolio optimization. **Our Portfolio Managers** * Are detail oriented * Work independently in an extremely fast\-paced environment with a high degree of ambiguity. Key job responsibilities * Review, audit and process rent in line with internal Spend \& Transaction Policies and contractual terms to ensure landlord/vendor adherence * Responsible for creating and managing weekly, monthly or quarterly vendor and partner reviews * Maintain positive relationships with internal stakeholders and deliver results requiring collaboration and coordination with multiple teams * Onboarding and tracking of payment setup for all new landlords/vendors * Interpret complex commercial contract language related to contractual obligations, SLAs, rent, tax, insurance and maintenance clauses * Review of contract related documentation and abstraction of critical information. * Assist leased team with various other administrative tasks **About The Team** Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, , and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, , and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in Business, Management, Real Estate, or another related field * Experience utilizing commercial real estate software platforms such as Tango, Yardi, or MRI **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience reading and interpreting lease language, managing critical lease dates, and conducting lease negotiations * Experience with lease administration processes * Experience in commercial real estate or lease administration within an industrial, commercial, and/or data center environment Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner. **Company** \- Amazon Data Services Ireland Limited Job ID: A10380087

title: Presales Solutions Consultant
description: **Modern Workplace Solutions Expert – AV \& Collaboration** **Dublin (Hybrid) \| Up to €55,000 (€73\.5k OTE) \+ Extensive Benefits** We’re working with a large international IT services and solutions organisation delivering infrastructure, cloud, workplace, and managed services solutions to enterprise and public\-sector customers across multiple markets. With continued investment in Modern Workplace technologies, the business is expanding its collaboration and hybrid working capability across Ireland and the UK. This is a customer\-facing pre\-sales and consultancy role focused on Modern Workplace, AV, and collaboration technologies. Working closely with Account Managers, you’ll engage customers to understand workplace challenges, design end\-to\-end collaboration solutions, and lead projects centred around meeting rooms, hybrid working, and workplace transformation initiatives. **Why apply:** * **High\-growth technology area:** Work across Modern Workplace, AV, collaboration, and hybrid working solutions * **Strategic customer exposure:** Deliver workshops, demos, and whiteboarding sessions with senior stakeholders * **Vendor\-led environment:** Gain exposure to leading technologies including Microsoft, Logitech, Neat, and Shure * **Strong progression opportunities:** Structured training, mentorship, and certification pathways * **International collaboration:** Support customers and initiatives across both Ireland and the UK * **Extensive benefits package:** Hybrid working, healthcare, incentives, modern tech setup, and enhanced annual leave - **What we’re looking for:** * **Pre\-sales or consultancy experience:** Background in IT solutions, AV, or Modern Workplace environments * **Modern Workplace technologies:** Experience with Teams Rooms, meeting room AV, signage, or hybrid collaboration solutions * **Customer engagement skills:** Ability to deliver workshops, demos, and solution presentations * **Solution design capability:** Experience scoping and designing end\-to\-end workplace solutions * **IT channel experience:** Exposure to reseller, VAR, MSP, or systems integration environments * **Vendor technology exposure:** Familiarity with technologies such as Microsoft Teams, Logitech, Neat, or Shure If you’re passionate about workplace transformation, collaboration technology, and customer engagement, this role offers the opportunity to work with leading vendors, influence strategic projects, and build a long\-term career within a growing international IT organisation.

title: Preschool Teacher
description: **Position starting in late August 2026** **Key Responsibilities**: \* Plan and deliver engaging, age\-appropriate activities and learning experiences. \* Create a safe, caring and inclusive environment where children can learn and thrive. \* Support children’s social, emotional, cognitive and physical development. \* Observe and monitor developmental progress and communicate effectively with parents/guardians. \* Build positive working relationships with colleagues, families and the wider school community. \* Ensure all health, safety and wellbeing standards are maintained at all times. \* Participate in ongoing professional development and training opportunities. **Essential Requirements**: \* Minimum QQI/FETAC Level 8 qualification (or equivalent) in Early Years Education. \* Previous experience working with young children, ideally in a preschool setting. \* Knowledge of Aistear and Síolta frameworks. \* Strong understanding of early childhood development and best practice. \* Excellent communication skills \- ability to converse clearly with children and adults in the setting. A high level of fluency in English is required. \* Excellent interpersonal skills and organisational skills. \* A warm, patient and enthusiastic approach to working with children. \* Ability to work both independently and collaboratively as part of a team. **Desirable Skills**: \* First Aid and Child Protection certification. \* Food and Manual Handling training desirable. \* Clear Garda Vetting (and International police clearance if having lived outside of Ireland for more than 6 months) is required. \* Two written references, one from your most recent employer, that will be verified. This is an excellent opportunity to join a supportive team and make a real difference in the lives of young learners. **Further Benefits:** \* 8% Holiday pay in final payroll \* Statutory sick pay \* CPD and career development opportunities \* EPV days \* Canteen with hot food **How to Apply:** Please email your CV and a cover letter outlining your relevant experience and suitability for the role to: ddix@rathdownschool.ie Please note that interviews will be conducted in person onsite at Rathdown Junior School. We are an equal opportunities employer and welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates, regardless of race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, disability, or age. We look forward to hearing from you. Job Types: Full\-time, Fixed term Contract length: 10 months Pay: €15\.00\-€17\.50 per hour Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * Dublin, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Education: * Bachelor's (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Product Growth Hacker (Exchange)
description: Crypto.com is on a mission to accelerate the world’s transition to cryptocurrency. As we continue to scale our Exchange App, we’re hiring a Growth Marketing Hacker to lead on our go\-to\-market strategy, lifecycle initiatives, and product positioning that fuel global user growth and engagement as we expand. This role sits at the intersection of product, data, and creative execution: ideal for a strategic marketer who thrives in fast\-paced, high\-ownership environments. You’ll work in close collaboration with product managers and growth teams to align feature rollouts, growth campaigns, and user insights into compelling narratives that resonate across geographies and user segments. ### **Responsibilities:** 1\. Strategy \& Positioning * Partner up with product and strategy teams to own the growth strategy for the Exchange (Advanced) vertical. * Develop stories and campaigns that differentiate our platform in a competitive global market. * Partner with data, analytics and product to define user personas (e.g. retail traders, crypto newcomers, high\-frequency traders) and map tailored journeys for each. * Lead on creative/copy positioning and brief submission for growth teams to deliver impactful and localised campaigns. * Work closely with product managers to align on roadmap priorities and customer feedback loops. 2\. Launch Execution * Partner up with the product team to manage the go\-to\-market planning for feature launches. * Conceptualise integrated marketing campaigns across in\-app, email, web, social, and earned media for UA to execute. * Partner with marketing, growth and product teams to ensure campaigns are timely, scalable, and data\-informed. * Coordinate with local market teams and growth to adapt messaging for region\-specific rollouts: localisation across product messaging, local behaviour and competitive landscape. 3\. Customer Insights \& Market Intelligence * Analyze market trends and conduct competitive benchmarking to identify opportunities and threats. * Run user interviews, feedback loops, and surveys to understand pain points and improve UX/messaging. * Feed findings back into product and growth teams to inform prioritization and positioning. 4\. Growth \& Retention * Design lifecycle programs that drive user activation, trading volume, and long\-term retention. * Conceptualise promotions, streaks, and incentive programs that reward engagement and boost retention. * Work with data, analytics and product teams to identify drop\-off points and optimize onboarding and reactivation flows. 5\. Performance \& Reporting * Work with Growth and Product to define KPIs across funnel metrics, retention, and campaign impact. * Report using tools like GA4, Tableau, Mixpanel: work closely with analytics and product to iterate quickly. ### **Requirements:** * 7\+ years of experience in product marketing: ideally crypto or financial background * Proven success launching and scaling B2C products or platforms and growing adoption and revenue sustainably. * Excellent communication and copywriting skills: you can simplify complex ideas and tell stories that convert. * Highly collaborative, with experience working cross\-functionally in fast\-paced environments. Bias to action. * Analytical mindset with proficiency in marketing analytics and user research. * Experience in regulated industries or financial services is a plus. We may use artificial intelligence tools to analyze the content of your Resume/CV against the specific requirements for the position. The purpose is to support our recruitment team in reviewing applications more effectively. These tools assist our recruitment team in their evaluation of your application by providing recommendations, but they do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans who consider the insights generated by the tools along with other relevant information. If you would like more details about how your personal information is processed, please contact us.

title: Project Controls
description: Winthrop Technologies is Europe’s only dedicated data centre delivery partner, offering full turnkey solutions from design through to construction and commissioning. Headquartered in Dublin, we deliver projects across Europe and have become the most trusted name in our sector. We are seeking a Junior Power BI Developer to join our growing data and digital function. This role is suited to a candidate with approximately 2\+ years’ experience in business intelligence or data analytics who is looking to further develop their skills in a fast\-paced construction and engineering environment. You will work closely with teams across project controls, commercial, finance, and operations to develop insightful dashboards and reports that support data\-driven decision\-making across cutting\-edge data centre projects. **Key Responsibilities:** * Develop, maintain, and enhance Power BI dashboards and reports to support construction and data centre projects * Model, transform, and cleanse data using Power Query (M) and DAX * Combine data from multiple sources such as Excel, ERP systems, project management tools, and SQL databases * Collaborate with business stakeholders to gather requirements and translate them into clear, actionable visual reports * Support data quality, consistency, and governance across reporting outputs * Assist with automation of recurring and manual reporting processes * Maintain documentation for dashboards, datasets, and reporting processes * Apply and promote best practices in BI development and data visualisation **Requirements** **Requirements:** * 2\+ years’ experience in a Power BI Developer, Data Analyst, or BI\-related role * Degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Business Analytics, or a related discipline * Strong hands\-on experience with Power BI in a professional environment * Solid understanding of: Data modelling concepts, DAX and Power Query (M), SQL or other data querying tools (desirable) * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with high attention to detail * Ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Interest in working within construction, engineering, or data centre infrastructure projects * Experience with integrating AI (Copilot) with Power BI semantic models for Q\&A across reporting. **Benefits** * Opportunity to work on Europe’s largest and most advanced data centre projects. * Career growth and professional development in a fast\-expanding sector. * Competitive salary package. * Supportive, dynamic, and collaborative team culture.

title: Project Delivery Engineer
description: **Project Delivery Engineer** **Overview** We are currently looking for a Project Delivery Engineer, with a strong electrical bias, to join our expanding team as we enter a new and exciting growth phase. The successful candidate will be responsible for managing the delivery of energy infrastructure, such as onsite generators and battery storage units, behind the meter at large commercial and industrial locations. We are looking for someone with experience in delivering energy, electrical or other comparable projects, with experience of doing so at industrial or commercial locations being advantageous. A highly motivated, self\-driven individual who understands how to conduct effective procurement processes, is familiar with electrical design and has strong project management capabilities The successful candidate will work closely with the Commercial team, to support early\-stage opportunity identification and appraisal, undertake site visits, produce high level technical proposals, engage and maintain relationships with sub\-contractors and delivery partners while ensuring projects are delivered on time, within scope and budget, while meeting safety and quality standards. **Who we are and w****hat we do:** VIOTAS is a market\-leading smart grid technology and services company headquartered in Limerick with offices in Melbourne, Krakow and Texas. Through us, large electricity customers actively participate in the electricity market to generate revenue in return for providing valuable services that help support the reliability of the power grid. Together, our clients can provide more power to the grid than some of the country’s large power stations. These services are delivered via our own custom designed control and communication systems. Our state\-of\-the\-art systems are designed to provide the fast frequency response services that are needed to facilitate increased integration of renewable energy on the power grid. The company was named the fastest growing technology company in Ireland at the Deloitte Fast 50 Awards in 2019\. We are expanding globally, with plans to implement energy system services in a greater number of other countries. **Responsibilities** * The Project Management of the end\-to\-end delivery of Behind the Meter asset development from inception through to handover. * Support effective and efficient procurement exercises to ensure we achieve best possible outcomes in terms of value and quality. * Support contract negotiations, including terms, scope, and commercial conditions. * Supporting early\-stage opportunity identification, undertaking site visits, conducting technical appraisals and producing technical solutions to the client’s satisfaction. * Coordinating with internal teams, subcontractors, suppliers, and clients to ensure timely and cost\-effective project delivery. * Support the continuous improvement of internal processes and frameworks to mature VIOTAS’ project development and delivery capabilities. * Manage and report on project schedules, milestones, and deliverables. * Support the procurement strategy including supplier engagement and selection * Conduct regular progress reviews, risk assessments, and mitigation planning. * Ensure all work complies with relevant regulations, standards, and policies. * Oversee complete commissioning of assets, including any necessary dispatch testing and / or grid code compliance testing * Liaise with clients, consultants, and third parties to maintain strong relationships and ensure customer satisfaction. * Liaising with other functions within VIOTAS – leveraging deep cross\-functional collaboration – as required to ensure projects are delivered to the client’s satisfaction. * Ensure all project documentation is completed in accordance with contractual obligations. * Prepare and present project reports to senior management and stakeholders **Essential Requirements:** * A minimum of four years previous relevant experience * A strong electrical background with a deep understanding of electrical equipment / infrastructure / generation equipment * Strong project management capabilities * Exceptional attention to detail and a pragmatic approach. Ability to work on own initiative with a high level of personal motivation. **Desirable:** * A project management qualification (PMP certification or equivalent) * A degree in electrical engineering or an equivalent qualification **Job Type:**Full\-Time, Permanent. **Location:** Limerick or Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid) **Salary:** Competitive based on qualifications, experience, and skills. **Benefits:** * Healthcare * Pension * Self\-Directed Training Budget of €3,000 per year plus 10 days training leave * Annual performance\-based bonus. * 25 days annual leave. * Death\-In\-Service benefit. * Dedicated People \& Culture club **Working at VIOTAS:** At VIOTAS, we foster growth, empower with autonomy, and cultivate an inclusive, flexible work culture. Our diverse, skilled team is dedicated to pioneering innovation and excellence, fuelled by a passion for a low\-carbon future. VIOTAS prioritise ongoing training and personal development, fostering a culture that values upskilling with dedicated budgetary support. We deeply value the well\-being of our employees, reflected in a range of initiatives designed to ensure their success and fulfilment. VIOTAS ranked top 15 in Ireland’s Best Workplaces, and also Best Workplaces in Tech in 2023\.

title: Project Engineer
description: **Position Description** The successful candidate will assume a role as project engineer coordinating and manage improvement projects for fisheries assets across company’s Hydroelectric Power Stations. The role is within the Operations team in Sustainable Rivers organization within Generation \& Trading. The Project Engineer is responsible for the safe, efficient, reliable management of all improvement projects outlined in the Sustainable Rivers Strategy. They will be a team player and self\-starter with the ability to multi\-task as well as meet deadlines. They will also have a demonstrable ability to communicate and relate effectively with staff at all levels in the organization. **Key Responsibilities** • Manage engineering projects from initial concept through design, procurement, construction, commissioning, and handover. • Develop and manage project scopes, specifications, schedules, budgets, and risk registers. • Ensure projects are delivered in line with agreed time, cost, quality, and performance targets. • Prepare technical evaluations and support tendering, contractor selection, and contract award processes. • Contractor management including site inspection and site supervision. • Review of Method Statements and Risk Assessments and other associated H\&S documentation required before work commences. • Contribute to review and upkeep of associated drawing and document management of projects. • Co\-ordinate the delivery of the project within the Hydropower stations and the interface between the contractor and the station staff. • Ensure all project activities comply with Health, Safety, Environmental, and Quality legislation, standards, and company procedures. • Actively promote and support a strong safety culture across projects and contractor activities. **Knowledge, Skills and Experience** **Essential** • Degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, Civil, or relevant discipline). • Demonstrated experience in engineering project delivery • Strong understanding of project management principles. • Knowledge of Health, Safety and Environmental legislation and best practice. • Excellent communication, stakeholder engagement, and report\-writing skills. • Strong IT skills (MS Office and engineering/project systems as applicable). **Desirable** • Knowledge of power generation, utilities, or similar regulated environments. **Qualifications** **Essential** • Engineering or equivalent degree • A full driving license • Flexibility to travel nationally is a requirement of this role **Anticipated start date: June 2026** **Duration 18months**

title: Project P6 Scheduler – Project Management Office (PMO)
description: **Project P6 Scheduler** **Location:** Dublin, Cork, Galway. **Join Us In Shaping The Future Of Project Delivery** Join a leading team where your planning expertise will help shape the successful delivery of complex engineering and infrastructure projects. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced Primavera P6 Scheduler to play a key role within a Project Management Office (PMO), supporting project managers and contributing directly to improved schedule performance, risk management, and project outcomes. **Purpose \& Scope Of The Role** We are seeking a Primavera P6 Scheduler to join the Project Management Office (PMO) and support Project Managers across a range of business sectors. The successful candidate will be responsible for assisting with the efficient delivery of projects and programmes, with particular focus on schedule management, risk, resourcing, and cost control. The role involves developing, maintaining, and analysing project schedules for multi\-disciplinary design projects from concept through to construction and handover. It also includes review and integration of contractor and supplier programmes into master project schedules, supporting reporting requirements, and helping to identify and implement corrective actions where required. **Your Impact In This Position** In this role, you will make a meaningful contribution to the successful delivery of projects by ensuring schedules are accurate, robust, and aligned with project objectives. You will help Project Managers maintain control of time, cost, and resources, while also supporting risk planning and analysis. Your work will directly improve visibility of project performance, strengthen decision\-making, and support the timely delivery of complex programmes. You will also contribute to continuous improvement by analysing project trends, capturing lessons learned, and supporting planning training and workshops. **Core Capabilities** **Planning \& Scheduling** Strong knowledge of project planning and scheduling principles, methods, and tools. Ability to develop, coordinate, track, and manage project schedules, identify critical issues, and support schedule performance across individual and portfolio\-level projects. **Attention to Detail** High level of accuracy in reviewing, interrogating, and maintaining schedules, cost data, and reporting outputs. Able to spot issues early and ensure information is reliable and actionable. **Communication \& Stakeholder Engagement** Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to work effectively with Project Managers, Clients, Contractors, Consultants, and internal stakeholders in a collaborative environment. **Problem Solving \& Initiative** Proactive approach to identifying schedule risks and issues, recommending corrective actions, and supporting timely resolution of complex planning challenges. **Teamwork \& Self\-Management** Works well as part of a multi\-disciplinary team, with strong organisational skills, good time management, and the ability to self\-motivate and deliver to deadlines. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Relevant degree or higher diploma in project management, civil engineering, or a related discipline. * Minimum 5\+ years’ experience in project planning, scheduling, and control across road and rail projects in Ireland, varying in size and complexity. * Experience working in an engineering design consultancy in the UK or Ireland as a Primavera P6 Scheduler. Experience should include logic development, developing and maintaining baselines, critical path mapping / analysis, cost and resource\-loading and adjustments including levelling, reporting metrics including earned value, e.g. SPI (Schedule Performance Index) and resource / cashflow profiling. * Proven knowledge of programme, risk, and resource management across engineering projects. * Experience developing Basis of Schedule documentation to support project schedules. * Strong report writing skills and the ability to present schedule information clearly and professionally. * Experience assisting with planning training and facilitating planning workshops is beneficial. * Experience with quantitative risk analysis techniques is advantageous. * Strong technical experience in delivery of projects within engineering consultancies and/or related construction projects. * Demonstrated experience working within accredited Integrated Management Systems (IMS) \& Standards, including quality, data security, and risk. * Proficient in MS Office Suite, particularly MS Excel and MS PowerPoint. **Working Environment** This role is based in Ireland and will involve working closely with Project Managers and multi\-disciplinary teams across a range of sectors. The position requires collaboration with internal stakeholders, Clients, Consultants, and Contractors. Flexibility to support project needs across different locations and working arrangements may be required, depending on project demands. Hybrid, remote, or flexible working arrangements can be incorporated where business and project requirements allow. **Why join Tetra Tech?** Tetra Tech is a leading provider of consulting and engineering services working across the full project lifecycle worldwide. We have a wide range of expertise across our teams, providing a global support network with a personalised approach to client relationships to better understand where we can add value. We provide a collaborative environment that supports individual performance, innovation, and creativity. W e support public and private sector clients on local, national, and international projects to deliver sustainable and resilient solutions. Our operations in the UK, Ireland, and the Netherlands include more than 6,000 employees who are Leading with Science® to solve our clients’ most complex problems. In alignment with the Equality Act 2010, we will make reasonable adjustments to support candidates and employees requiring additional arrangements. This could include adaptations to work schedules, training approaches, or the physical workspace. Please inform us if you need any accommodations during the recruitment process or in your day\-to\-day role. **Recruitment Agencies** We have a Preferred Supplier List of trusted partners to assist us when required and do not acknowledge any speculative CVs or unsolicited candidate introductions from agencies not on this list and any submitted will be taken as a gift. Please refer to the careers page on our website if you require any further information.

title: Quality Analyst, Albanian Speaker - Video Safety Operations, T&S
description: **Responsibilities** About The Team Our QA team performs a critical function that supports our efforts to address objectionable or disturbing content. Content QA interacts with includes images, video, text related to every day life, but they can also include (but are not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child abuse; sexual assault; torture; bestiality; self\-harm; suicide; murder. It is possible that this role will be exosed to harmful content on a daily basis. Responsibilities \- Perform quality assessments by reviewing and evaluating the accuracy and consistency of policy application for content in the Albanian market. \- Analyse quality data to identify root causes, trends, and areas for improvement, summarizing findings in a clear and actionable way. \- Prepare and share QA reports with Content Quality Center stakeholders, acting as a point of alignment to ensure consistent quality standards across operations. \- Collaborate closely with Local Operations and Trust \& Safety teams, serving as an escalation point for policy\-related questions, issues, and solution alignment. \- Participate in and, when needed, facilitate coaching and calibration sessions to support consistent policy interpretation and application. **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications \- At least 10 months of experience working with content quality, safety, or policy\-related activities. \- Strong understanding of European online safety, privacy, and freedom of expression topics. \- High attention to detail, with the ability to work independently and manage tasks with accuracy. \- Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment, adapting effectively to daily operational challenges. \- Resilience and a strong commitment to self\-care when handling emotionally demanding content. \- Flexibility to work different shifts, as required by the operation. \- High proficiency in the Albanian language, as this role supports the Albanian\-speaking market. Preferred Qualifications: \- Bachelor’s degree is preferred, however, not essential. \- Proven track record of working with co\-workers across different countries is preferable but not required. **Trust \& Safety** Content that this role interacts with includes images, video, and text related to every\-day life, but it can also include (but is not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child safety; depictions of harm to self and others, and harm to animals. Hence, it is possible that this role will be exposed to harmful content on a daily basis. ​ ​ TikTok recognises that keeping our platform safe for the TikTok communities is no ordinary job which can be both rewarding and psychologically demanding and emotionally taxing for some. This is why we are sharing the potential hazards, risks and implications in this unique line of work from the start, so our candidates are well informed before joining. ​ ​ We are committed to the wellbeing of all our employees and promise to provide comprehensive and evidence\-based programs, to promote and support physical and mental wellbeing throughout each employee's journey with us. We believe that wellbeing is a relationship and that everyone has a part to play, so we work in collaboration and consultation with our employees and across our functions in order to ensure a truly person\-centred, innovative and integrated approach.​ **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​ **Why Join Us** Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day.​ We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​ ​ Diversity \& Inclusion​ TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​
